title: On Site Mesa Self Storage Units Auction
description: Storage Unit Auction at Mesa Self Storage in Pueblo  13 Units as of today!!Tuesday March 3rd2003 Santa Fe AvenueRegistration Starts at 10 amAuction Starts at 11 amAs of publication of this advertisement we have 13 units that will be available at auction Tuesday the 3rd of March at 2003 Santa Fe Avenue at Mesa Self Storage out on the Mesa.  We will start at 11 a.m. and will be open for registration at 10 a.m. the same day.  No buyers premium but there will be a 3% fee for credit cards and tax unless you have a copy of your tax license.  You will be given until Sunday the 8th of March to clear the unit out.  Units must be swept clean by Sunday.  Bring a lock(s) for your units that you purchase.  Cash or credit card accepted.  Government issued i.d. must be presented for registration.  This will be a quick auction that will have some great deals.  Auction starts at 11 a.m..  If you have any questions do not contact Mesa, contact Top Bid Auction through email at topbidauction@gmail.com or call John at 719.242.8928. "All items are sold 'as is' and 'where is' with no warranties, expressed or implied." "All sales are final. No refunds or exchanges will be permitted." "The auctioneer is not responsible for errors in bidding or descriptions." "Items must be removed from the premises by March 8th at 6p.m."

title: Store Goods, Overstock, Returns, Shelf Pulls #2426A
description:
Exciting Auction – Hundreds of Items, Something for Everyone!

Don't miss this massive liquidation auction packed with high-value, in-demand items across every category imaginable. Whether you're a bargain hunter, reseller, or savvy shopper, this is your chance to score big!

Electronics & Gaming – Lead the pack with an Xbox Series X 1TB SSD 4K console, SteelSeries Arena 7 2.1 RGB gaming speakers, Govee RGBIC permanent outdoor LED lights, and a Govee smart LED strip light system. Also featured: wireless mic systems, DJ laser lights, and a Versa Desk electric standing desk riser.

Home & Bedroom – Upgrade your space with a hybrid Full XL mattress, twin hybrid memory foam mattress, queen adjustable memory foam pillow, gray comforter sets, heated blankets, bedding sets, and mattress toppers.Kitchen & Appliances – Bid on a nugget ice maker, espresso machine, air fryer, roaster oven, electric juicer, yogurt maker, cheese grater, ceramic hot plate, and a stainless steel 102oz coffee carafe.

Health, Wellness & Mobility – Featuring a Tesla 48A EV Wall Connector charger, cold therapy knee unit, foot and calf massagers with heat, shiatsu neck massager, vibration plate fitness machine, rollator walker, raised toilet seats, and a bidet seat.

Tools & Automotive – Including a professional backpack vacuum, electric pressure washers, cordless leaf blower, cordless concrete vibrator, oil filter wrench set, serpentine belt tool kit, ratcheting wrench set, hydraulic pliers, wheel spacers, and multiple car seat covers, floor mats, headlights, and tail lights.

Outdoor & Pet – Grab solar security cameras, a pool leaf vacuum, sewage pump, pool filter, automatic pet feeders, self-cleaning litter box, dog crates, and a large elevated dog bath tub.

Décor & Furniture – Wall mirrors, chandeliers, bookshelves, rugs, curtains, window blinds, neon signs, framed art, and a 7.5ft pre-lit rotating Christmas tree.

All items sold as-is. All Sales Final. Bid now — deals like these don't last!

title: March 3, 2026 Consignment Auction
description:
Welcome to our March 3rd - Pre-Planting Consignment Online Auction!  You are welcome to browse through the catalog.  All items posted are located at the Wieman Auction Facility near Marion, SD.

There is no buyers' premium with this auction, but 4.2% South Dakota Sales Tax applies to all purchases.  

This is a fast-paced online auction with 1 lot closing every 25 seconds.  There is a soft close with 1 minute extension, meaning, if there is a bid in the last 60 seconds of a lot, 60 seconds will be added.

title: HB-3/3/26 - Where will silver go this time?
description:
About this auction-   It is 100% the bidders responsibility to be familiar with the terms used and the value is subjective and 100% the bidders due diligence responsibility without fail. The auction LOT Title and image are the definitive measure of each item. The auction title is a broad description of the items up for sale, and not the totality of the sale being offered.  We offer a reasonably fast shipping services and do NOT allow bidders to pick up items due to the nature of what we sell. Please note- the terminology " MAY HAVE WON" means that the sale isnt over on all the venues we are selling the goods on - The auctioneer record is the record of transaction.  You may still be outbid by other bidders.

1.) All items are sold as is , where is, with no refunds, discounts to bid, or refunds. 

2.) Bidder agrees that any bid placed will be honored as a contract and the credit card that you registered with will be charged by Antique Finder Services at the end of the sale.  If this is not available, we will send a  invoice that must be completed within 24 hours of receipt.  It is the responsibility of the bidder that the card  registered be able to complete the sale, or call us with an alternate means of payment within 24 hours of end of sale.

3.) Bidder agrees to pay a Buyer's Premium of 20% via Credit Card - 18% Via Paper Check or Bank Wire or ACH. All invoices more than $5000 will be required to be paid by check, bank wire or ACH -

4.) If for any reason you do not pay within 48 hours of end of auction, we will resell the items and the collected monies will go towards paying your invoice. Any shortages, you will still be liable for.

5.) Shipping will take place within 15 to 30 business days ( dependant upon where the physical sale takes place - Since we have more than one location, multiple shipments may arrive for individual invoices) from cleared payment into our bank account. Tracking will be emailed to the bidders email. We are not liable for shipping delays due to weather or press of business for the USPS or pick up delays. Bidder agrees to a NON CHARGEBACK transaction. If for ANY reason a client charges back - the original invoice, plus 100% fee will be charged to the clients account.  

6.)  All items will be described in detail and have at least 1 high resolution image for you to base your valuation on. We do not control the price of the item, and cannot accept returns, unless not as described. Example... you purchased an item and the incorrect item was sent to you. Such as 14k gold ring is not 14k gold. 

7.) Any and all legal recourse for either party will take place in Snohomish County Washington and we reserve the right to charge for the legal fees. Any liability by seller never is more than the original value of any invoice  Bidder agrees to a NON CHARGEBACK transaction. Shipping delays are common in the metals industry and are NOT a reason for a chargeback. We guarantee full and complete invoice shipping. We cannot control shipping times on some bullion items, or out of country delays. 


AUCTION TERMINOLOGY:

BU = Brilliant uncirculated  

ASE = American silver eagle 

NON BULLION = Means nothing further can be rendered from this product 

Gold Leaf = A composite of metals with very little gold content, hammered flat to a milimeter thickness to be used for gold leafing of articles -  These Gold Leaf Flakes are made from a Composition of scrap metal flakes from A Gold leafing manufacturer.  There is no melt value to these flakes and they are Not for precious metal investment. These Gold Flakes are NOT edible gold leaf. 

Gold Leaf Scrap = Gold leaf cuttings from the above mentioned product

Alluvial Gold= Gold from natural earth mining via streams or rocks 

Natural = From the earth with accepted treatments to bring out value and vibrance 

Electronically Tested = Means that the item will need to be further evaluated to confirm whether it is natural or man made. Electronically tested items have all the same chemical composition and elements as a natural item 

Random date = means random from the dates we have available at the time of the sale, with no guarantee as to specific dates -

BU and Proof Coins are coins from US mint and proof sets - and never in circulation -

description: Area Collectors #2 with over 600 lots, is a diverse multi-estate auction packed with quality collectibles, advertising, die-cast models, vintage décor, tools, sporting goods, and more. This extensive sale features an impressive lineup of 1/64 scale die-cast semis and farm models, airline and aviation replicas, Coca-Cola collectibles, Toby jugs, crocks, cookie jars, holiday décor, and unique advertising pieces. Highlights include a Seth Thomas Model 1819 wall clock, a Daisy pump BB gun, numerous Ertl and farm-related die-cast models, vintage toys, jewelry collections, Mary Kay memorabilia, furniture, electronics, and household goods. With something for collectors, decorators, hobbyists, and resellers alike, this auction offers a wide-ranging selection spanning vintage to modern items — don’t miss your opportunity to bid and add to your collection!

title: March Winter Estate Finds
description:
Our March Winter Estate Finds Auction features over 300 lots of artwork, jewelry, furniture, books, silver, and decorative and ethnographic items. 
There is a large selection of Art Deco furniture, decorative accents, and textiles including Chinese Art Deco rugs. Other furniture highlights include a LaVerne L’Etreinte Mirror and Les Chinois Cocktail Table. 
Collectors of Folk Art will find Hooked Rugs in a variety of patterns and an early American School Oil on Canvas. The sale also features a selection of Mid-Century advertising and pop culture memorabilia. Other highlights include ethnographic items such as a Native American Bow and Arrow and 2 Native American Hide War Shirts. 
Jewelry highlights include a 18k & Coral Beaded Necklace, a 14k Gold Nugget Bracelet, and a variety of sterling and beaded pieces. A large selection of books is also featured, with first editions and rare books, including 12 volumes of Freud’s Imago and 6 volumes of his Journal of Psychoanalysis.

title: Luxe Lots: Porcelain, Decor & Estate Treasures
description:
uxe Lots: Porcelain, Decor & Estate Treasures
Step into a beautifully curated collection of refined estate finds in our Luxe Lots auction. This catalog showcases an elegant mix of fine porcelain, European decorative arts, and timeless pieces designed to elevate any collection or interior.

Featured throughout the sale are quality examples of European porcelain, including hand-painted and highly detailed works such as Delft, KPM, and other respected makers. From decorative plates and figurines to statement pieces, this auction offers both seasoned collectors and new buyers an opportunity to acquire classic designs with enduring appeal.

You will also find a variety of decorative accents and estate treasures—perfect for adding character, sophistication, and charm to your home or resale inventory. Whether you are searching for a standout display piece or a subtle addition to your collection, this auction delivers a refined selection with broad appeal.

 Highlights Include:
Fine European porcelain & decorative pieces
Hand-painted and intricately detailed works
Estate-fresh decorative accessories
Classic designs with strong collector interest
All items come from estate collections and have been carefully selected to offer quality, beauty, and value.
Bidding Information
Bidding is open now—don’t wait until the last minute! Items begin closing at the scheduled time, with lots ending every 20 seconds. Be sure to place your maximum bids in advance to ensure you don’t miss out.

Shipping & Pickup
Shipping is handled by a third-party professional shipper. Please refer to our terms for full details. Local pickup is available during posted hours.

Don’t miss your chance to bring home something special—happy bidding!

title: Dear Diamonds And Jewelry Auction Ends 03/03/2026
description:
READ BELOW - Important Auction Information

HOW DOES THIS WORK? It's simple. Dear Diamonds and Jewelry is an online public auction company specializing in jewelry, antique coins and other rarities. These items are offered for sale to the public on our website for a fixed period of time with a defined closing date and time. To register you simply sign up for an account on our secure website. Your information is validated and then you may begin placing your bids!

HOW DO I REGISTER? At the top of your screen, click "Login/New Bidder." If you are a new bidder, click the black box. This will prompt you to enter your email address. You will be emailed a link to validate your email, and then you can finish your registration. It's fast, easy and secure. The whole process takes about 2 minutes. Your credit card will be validated by and in doing so, a temporary hold of $1 will be placed on your account to ensure the credit card is valid, and that only legitimate bidders are participating. This process is exactly like paying for gas at the pump! The charge will fall off of your account in 24-48 hours.

HOW DOES THE AUCTION WORK? The auction is open for bidding as soon as it goes "LIVE" online. Bidding is super easy, at any time you can begin placing your bids and may enter a maximum bid.

title: UsedTwo Auction 80
description:
Welcome to UsedTwo Auctions. Preview images of catalog items will become available as upcoming auctions are processed. The complete Auction Catalog, including all preview images, will be available by Monday evening prior to the commencement of the auction.

In-person preview appointments are available Tuesday through Thursday from 10:00 a.m. to 2:00 p.m. during the active auction period. Previews will be conducted at our Frederick warehouse, situated at 7313 Grove Road, Warehouse Unit G, Frederick, MD 21704. Please contact 301-685-5677 to confirm appointment availability.

Auctions operate from Tuesday to Tuesday. Item collection is scheduled for the Thursday following auction closure, from 10:00 AM to 6:00 PM. In the event that Thursday pickup is not feasible, please contact us via email at team@usedtwo.com prior to auction close to arrange an alternative pickup time. Items remaining uncollected by the close of business on the following Thursday, for which no rescheduling arrangement has been communicated, may be forfeited. UsedTwo disclaims liability for any items not retrieved within the designated pickup timeframe.

Shipping/Pickup Information

We do not provide shipping services. However, clients may utilize our local UPS shipping service for item pickup, packing, and shipping.

Important Notice:

Items must be scheduled for pickup no later than 9 AM on the Friday following the auction's conclusion. UPS will collect items on the Friday following the auction's close, provided all required documentation has been submitted.

Documentation Requirements:
A photograph of the signed invoice.
A photograph of the driver's license with matching address.
Both documents must be emailed prior to the release of items for shipping to team@usedtwo.com and The UPS Store at store5681@theupsstore.com. Failure to provide the required documentation will result in the items not being released, invoices not being refunded, and forfeiture of the items. Packing services are provided exclusively by UPS. Clients are required to utilize our designated local UPS Store for pickup, packing, and shipping services. Prepaid shipping labels are not accepted. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the address on file with Hibid must be submitted to The UPS Store before items can be released for shipping and packing. For out-of-state clients and those purchasing jewelry, gold, silver, or other items at our discretion, additional requirements apply: 1. A copy of the invoice and a shipping address matching the Hibid address must be submitted to The UPS Store. 2. A valid driver's license (photo or scan) with an address matching the Hibid address must be provided. 3. The paid invoice (attached to the winning bidder email) must be signed, photographed/scanned, and emailed back to us. Release of items to UPS pickup is contingent upon receipt of all required documents.

To schedule a pickup time and date, please email team@usedtwo.com, specifying that items should be collected from UsedTwo Auctions, 7313 Grove Road Unit G, Frederick MD 21704. Contact information for The UPS Store is as follows:

Your Team at The UPS Store
5257 Buckeystown Pike
Frederick, MD 21704
Phone: 301-620-8825 Fax: 301-620-8821
Website: https://www.theupsstore.com/5681
Email: store5681@theupsstore.com

Subscribe to our Newsletter and follow us on Facebook to become a UsedTwo Auction Insider, gaining access to pre-auction Sneak Peeks and more.

Please ensure UsedTwo communications are not directed to your spam folder. We aim to promptly notify you of winning bids.

For any questions or concerns, please email team@usedtwo.com.

title: TUE ANTIQUE FRENCH QUALITY FURNITURE COPPER DEPT 56 COINS
description:
FINAL: PREVIEW IS FRI, MON, TUE 9-5 CLOSED 12-1 PUIS WED, THU, FRI SAT & SUN 10-5 CLOSED 12-1

WE SHIP IN HOUSE.  THIS MEANS BETTER RATES, FASTER TURN OVER FOR SHIPPING.  YOU WILL RECEIVE A TEXT MESSAGE OR EMAIL POST AUCTION.  IF YOU NEED SHIPPING FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS.

PLEASE PICKUP ITEMS BY END OF DAY SUNDAY 5PM FOLLOWING THE AUCTION.  

IF YOU CAN NOT THERE ARE OPTIONS FOR SMALLER ITEMS. FURNITURE WILL NOT BE ALLOWED FOR LATE PICKUP AS WE HAVE LIMITED SPACE.

1. EMAIL AT TYLERGRACEAUCTIONS@GMAIL.COM AND ASK FOR A LATE PICKUP. 

2. REQUEST SHIPPING THROUGH LINKS PROVIDED

3. CONTACT A MOVER OF YOURS OR A RECOMMENDED MOVER AT THE BOTTOM OF YOUR INVOICE PAGE.

IF YOU DO NONE OF THESE AND JUST ABANDON YOUR ITEMS THEY WILL BE RELISTED AND YOUR ITEMS AND MONIES WILL BE FORFEITED.

SHIPPING 

THERE WILL BE A LINK PROVIDED IN THE EMAIL AND OR TEXT MESSAGES FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS PROVIDED.

AFTER 48 HOURS IF NO RESPONSE WE WILL JUST SHIP USING THE CREDIT CARD ON FILE, BUT THIS SLOWS THE PROCESS DOWN, YOU DONT GET TEXT MESSAGES WITH TRACKING UPDATES BECAUSE YOU DIDNT CLICK THE LINK SENT TO YOU.   BUT WE CAN NOT HOLD ONTO ITEMS WAITING ON THAT PROCESS.   

IF NO CONTACT HAS BEEN MADE AND THE PAYMENT DOESNT GO THROUGH FOR SHIPPING, NORMAL ABANDONMENT TIMES AND FORFEITURE POLICIES WILL APPLY.

PAYMENT

I REQUIRE A GOOD CREDIT CARD ON FILE TO BID THAT CAN BE CHARGED THE NIGHT OF AUCTION.   IN THE EVENT A CREDIT CARD DOESN'T GO THROUGH THE NIGHT OF AUCTION THERE WILL BE A LINK SENT OVER TO PAY VIA ZELLE.  IF THAT INVOICE IS NOT PAID THE NIGHT OF.  THERE WILL BE A $10 INCONVENIENCE FEE ADDED.  IF THE BALANCE IS NOT PAID FOR BIDDING PRIVILEGES WILL BE SUSPENDED IN HIBID.   

IF YOU ARE BIDDING ON GOLD OR POTENTIALLY OTHER EXPENSIVE ITEMS .  I WILL REQUIRE A ZELLE OR CASH PAYMENT ALWAYS IF YOU ARE A NEW BIDDER OR OUT OF STATE.   AND POSSIBLY IF YOU ARE LOCAL.   IT WILL BE AT THE AUCTIONEERS DISCRETION IF I CHOOSE NOT TO.   ESTABLISHED BIDDERS WILL NOT HAVE TO WORRY ABOUT THIS. 

WE DO NOT TAKE OUT OF STATE TAX PERMITS. AND WE CHECK VALIDITY OF IN STATE TAX PERMITS PRIOR TO REMOVING SALES TAX.

RECEIPT OF ITEMS AND SIGNING OF INVOICE.

MOST AUCTIONS HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  IN HOME ESTATES DO NOT HAVE THIS ABILITY AND THEREFORE YOU ARE BIDDING ON AN ITEM BASED ON THE PICTURES AND DESCRIPTION GIVEN KNOWING THAT AN ITEM IF NOT LISTED AS NEW MAY HAVE WEAR, TEAR, OR OTHER POSSIBLE DEFECTS.    THIS MEANS UNLESS I MAKE A MISTAKE SAY I LIST A TV AS A MICROWAVE, A TWIN BED AS A FULL OR SIMILAR OBVIOUS MISTAKE THEN ALL ITEMS ARE AS IS WHERE IS WITH NO WARRANTY EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.   

IF IT IS AT THE AUCTION HOUSE.   A PREVIEW IS ALWAYS OFFERED, THERE IS NO EXCUSE FOR NOT KNOWING THE CONDITION OF AN ITEM.  IF THERE IS A DISCREPANCY THEN IT NEEDS TO BE DISCUSSED PRIOR TO BEING TAKEN OFF PREMISES.   ONCE THE INVOICE IS SIGNED YOU ARE AGREEING THAT THE ITEM IS ACCEPTABLE IN ITS DESCRIPTION AND FORM AND NO FURTHER DISCUSSION WILL OCCUR ON THE MATTER.   ULTIMATELY I AM THE FINAL SAY ON THE MATTER AND RESERVE THAT RIGHT.

IF YOU ARE BIDDING AND REQUIRE SHIPPING AND YOU DO NOT HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  YOU ARE BIDDING ON THE ITEM IN A BIT OF A BLIND FORMAT.  YOU ARE ACCEPTING THE ITEM BASED SOLELY ON THE PICTURES PROVIDED AND DESCRIPTION.  AGAIN IN THIS CASE YOU ARE RECOGNIZING THAT UNLESS AN ITEM IS DESCRIBED AS NEW THEN THERE MAY BE INTERNAL EXTERNAL FLAWS AND SIGNS OF USAGE AND YOU ARE BIDDING ON THESE ITEMS AS IS WHERE IS NO WARRANTIES ARE EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.

I RESERVE THE RIGHT TO BE FINAL AUTHORITY ON ANY DISCREPANCY OR ISSUE THAT MAY COME ABOUT IN THE COURSE OF THE AUCTION PROCESS.

title: Commercial Building for Sale in Abingdon VA
description:
Commercial Building for Sale in Abingdon VA
Looking for a spot to start your next business venture? Check out this solid steel Red Iron building constructed in 2020, that sits on 0.77 acres. It is designed for long-lasting durability and low maintenance. With its many desirable features, the property is move-in ready or can be easily modified to suit your specific business needs.
The building offers approximately 1,600 +/- sq. ft. and is zoned B-2 in Washington County. Features include 3 HVAC split-system units, full insulation, LED lighting throughout, an apartment-size kitchen, full bathroom, washer and dryer hookups, and handicap accessibility. The building sits on a solid foundation, and the floors were professionally epoxy-coated three years ago.
Conveniently located just outside the town limits, the property still benefits from town water and sewer. Outside, there is an older garage building that could be restored for additional storage, along with space for 10+ parking spots.
This is an excellent opportunity for a governmental office, professional services, or general commercial retail business. Don’t miss your chance to secure a versatile commercial property with outstanding potential!
There is a 15’ Private Maintenance Agreement made between the Town of Abingdon and the subject property. See bidder packet for more information.
Online Auction Ends Wednesday, March 4th at 4 PM
19462 Lee Highway, Abingdon, VA 24210 
PREVIEW DATE: Saturday, February 21st at 11 AM
CONTACT: Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at 540-239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com. You can also contact Realtor Barbara Cordle at 276-701-3445 or email bjcordle@gmail.com
DIRECTIONS: From Exit Ramp 14 off I-81 Turn right onto Old Jonesboro Rd. toward Abingdon. Go for 0.4 mi. Turn left onto Lee Hwy (US-11/US-19) toward Bristol. Property will be located on the left, marked by sign.
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 4 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating VA State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property information.
TERMS AND CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $10,000 is required on the day of sale. Close on or before April 20th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the Seller up to, but not beyond the Seller’s reserve price. Auction starts ending at 4 PM on March 4th, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. Be sure to register to bid early to ensure you receive notifications regarding any changes to the Auction, Bidder Packet and/or Pre-Auction offers received. Download Bidder Information Pack for complete terms and conditions, purchase contract and maps at BidBlueRidge.com

Once items are received, CRS will contact each buyer directly to confirm shipping arrangements and provide a shipping quote.Shipping quotes are calculated based on the weight and dimensions of each item. title: Bay Harbor Island Estate Auction
description:
This auction presents the lifelong collection of a well-traveled connoisseur with a sophisticated eye for the unique and the historical. Reflecting a life spent exploring global markets and fine estates, the catalog features a worldly blend of musical history, sacred art, and high-end decorative treasures.

The heart of the estate includes an expansive library of Rock ‘n’ Roll records, ranging from definitive classic rock staples to rare pressings curated by a true student of the genre. Complementing this musical archive is a stunning array of religious icons and Asian art pieces that represent various traditions and cultures, each chosen for their exceptional craftsmanship, spiritual depth, and aesthetic beauty. Beyond the fine art, the auction offers a diverse selection of rarities, jewelry and estate finds that highlight the incredible breadth of a seasoned collector’s journey.

title: 03-04-2026 Lifetime Pabst Blue Ribbon & Breweriana Auction
description:
Lifetime Pabst Blue Ribbon and Breweriana Collection!

A tremendous and terrific collection of vintage and antique neon beer signs of various sizes and designs, rare metal and plastic displays, light-ups, dozens of wooden plaques and wall hangs, unique frames and signage, beer serving trays and tins, lamps, glassware, and an awesome gathering of beer collectibles with a huge focus in particular on Pabst Blue Ribbon merchandise. 

Interesting and intriguing items in working condition and well-preseved plus some huge and very vintage metal promotional beer displays, two Jagermeister Tap Machines, PBR mirrors and displays, numerous light-ups, clocks and displays, and tons of treasures focusing on the History of Beer Promotions and yesteryear advertising! 

Online Auction Wednesday March 4, 2026
Bid Now! ww.tomhallauctions.com

Preview by appointment, call 610-799-0808
Items are located at Tom Hall Auction Gallery, 4644 Rt. 309, Schnecksville, Pa. 18078

Pick up hours at the gallery following the auction:  
12noon-3:30pm the day after the auction and Monday thru Friday from 9:30AM-3:30PM  daily.

All items MUST be removed within 7 DAYS of the auction.

Local deliver available. Please call us at 610-799-0808 for information.

Online Only Bidding: Auction starts closing with 2 items closing per minute, bidding continues as auction closes.

Extended bidding, if a bid is placed within the last 1 minute of a lot’s scheduled close time the bidding will be extended 1 minute from the last bid.

Buying at auction is not like buying new; the items are used, vintage and antique. They are consigned by estates and people downsizing. They have been used in people’s homes for their purposes, so while most items are in good condition, most are not in perfect condition. We provide private previews for the public to come to the gallery and physically inspect items being sold. If you cannot be here for the inspection, please contact us by email for additional photos or more detailed condition reports.
We try to present items to bidders fairly; however, it is the bidder’s duty to make independent investigation of the condition of items before bidding.

title: Eagle Mountain Estate Part 2, Mid Century Collections
description:
Shipping, Delivery, & Local Pickup are available 
Auction begins Monday, Februaty 23rd at 6:00PM. Central Standard Time 
Auction ends Wednesday, March 4th at 7:00 P.M. Central Standard Time
Items begin closing at 7:00 P.M. and will close at a rate of three per minute. 
There will be a 3-Day Pick Up for items won:
Thursday, March 5th from 1:00 P.M. to 5:00 P.M. 
Friday, March 6th from 10:00 A.M. to 5:00 P.M.
Saturday, March 7th from 10:00 A.M. to Noon
Winning bidders will be contacted by email with their invoice and pick-up instructions following the completion of this auction.  Please look for an email at the completion of the auction. All instructions will be in the invoiced email sent out to the winners.  The email will come from Live Oak Auctions.  We usually have invoices sent out close to an hour after the last bid of the auction.
Please contact us if you have any questions at 682-327-9247 or 817-205-2888!  Text message is our best means of communication, especially after business hours!  Thank you!  
It’s another Mid Century Bonanza with list after list of collectibles in so many categories that we aren’t going to be able to “list” them all.  Check out that Pink Poodle Skirt in Lot 242 from the Elvis and Malt Shop days of the 1950’s!  How about a 1940’s-1950’s cruise down Rockaway on that Ross Chain Beach Bike in Lot 44?  Who needs Retro when you can have the REAL DEAL?  
You’ve got 10 days from opening on February 23rd to get your list ready for another fast-paced mid-century auction that will be closing next Wednesday evening on March 4th.  Other items to mention that will be sure to add to your collections, or start new ones!
- 2 Project Cars including a 1975 Ford Mustang and a 1995 Mitsubishi Eclipse
- Mid Century Era Radios, Stereo Equipment, VHS Video Cassette Players, 8 Track Tapes (and newer) DVD players
- Mid Century Era Board Games (and newer) Hot Wheels Collections, we have 100s and 100s and they are all still in their original factory packages!
- Golf Bags w/ Assorted Clubs, like we have EIGHT of these for you to choose from, plus a Golf Bag Caddy, Golf Accessories, and Golf Decor.  Even Santa is going for birdie in Lot 248.
- Camping Equipment
- We have TOOLS, TOOLS, TOOLS.  And more TOOLS.  Some are Vintage, many are Newer, and a lot are Craftsman.
Are you having fun yet?  We certainly hope so!  And we hope to see you pop up over and over again on auction night.  Good Luck from LOA on your highest bids!  See you after the auction at pick up in Fort Worth, Texas!

PLEASE PICKUP ITEMS BY END OF DAY SUNDAY 5PM FOLLOWING THE AUCTION.  

IF YOU CAN NOT THERE ARE OPTIONS FOR SMALLER ITEMS. FURNITURE WILL NOT BE ALLOWED FOR LATE PICKUP AS WE HAVE LIMITED SPACE.

1. EMAIL AT TYLERGRACEAUCTIONS@GMAIL.COM AND ASK FOR A LATE PICKUP. 

2. REQUEST SHIPPING THROUGH LINKS PROVIDED

3. CONTACT A MOVER OF YOURS OR A RECOMMENDED MOVER AT THE BOTTOM OF YOUR INVOICE PAGE.

IF YOU DO NONE OF THESE AND JUST ABANDON YOUR ITEMS THEY WILL BE RELISTED AND YOUR ITEMS AND MONIES WILL BE FORFEITED.

SHIPPING 

THERE WILL BE A LINK PROVIDED IN THE EMAIL AND OR TEXT MESSAGES FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS PROVIDED.

AFTER 48 HOURS IF NO RESPONSE WE WILL JUST SHIP USING THE CREDIT CARD ON FILE, BUT THIS SLOWS THE PROCESS DOWN, YOU DONT GET TEXT MESSAGES WITH TRACKING UPDATES BECAUSE YOU DIDNT CLICK THE LINK SENT TO YOU.   BUT WE CAN NOT HOLD ONTO ITEMS WAITING ON THAT PROCESS.   

IF NO CONTACT HAS BEEN MADE AND THE PAYMENT DOESNT GO THROUGH FOR SHIPPING, NORMAL ABANDONMENT TIMES AND FORFEITURE POLICIES WILL APPLY.

PAYMENT

I REQUIRE A GOOD CREDIT CARD ON FILE TO BID THAT CAN BE CHARGED THE NIGHT OF AUCTION.   IN THE EVENT A CREDIT CARD DOESN'T GO THROUGH THE NIGHT OF AUCTION THERE WILL BE A LINK SENT OVER TO PAY VIA ZELLE.  IF THAT INVOICE IS NOT PAID THE NIGHT OF.  THERE WILL BE A $10 INCONVENIENCE FEE ADDED.  IF THE BALANCE IS NOT PAID FOR BIDDING PRIVILEGES WILL BE SUSPENDED IN HIBID.   

IF YOU ARE BIDDING ON GOLD OR POTENTIALLY OTHER EXPENSIVE ITEMS .  I WILL REQUIRE A ZELLE OR CASH PAYMENT ALWAYS IF YOU ARE A NEW BIDDER OR OUT OF STATE.   AND POSSIBLY IF YOU ARE LOCAL.   IT WILL BE AT THE AUCTIONEERS DISCRETION IF I CHOOSE NOT TO.   ESTABLISHED BIDDERS WILL NOT HAVE TO WORRY ABOUT THIS. 

WE DO NOT TAKE OUT OF STATE TAX PERMITS. AND WE CHECK VALIDITY OF IN STATE TAX PERMITS PRIOR TO REMOVING SALES TAX.

RECEIPT OF ITEMS AND SIGNING OF INVOICE.

MOST AUCTIONS HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  IN HOME ESTATES DO NOT HAVE THIS ABILITY AND THEREFORE YOU ARE BIDDING ON AN ITEM BASED ON THE PICTURES AND DESCRIPTION GIVEN KNOWING THAT AN ITEM IF NOT LISTED AS NEW MAY HAVE WEAR, TEAR, OR OTHER POSSIBLE DEFECTS.    THIS MEANS UNLESS I MAKE A MISTAKE SAY I LIST A TV AS A MICROWAVE, A TWIN BED AS A FULL OR SIMILAR OBVIOUS MISTAKE THEN ALL ITEMS ARE AS IS WHERE IS WITH NO WARRANTY EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.   

IF IT IS AT THE AUCTION HOUSE.   A PREVIEW IS ALWAYS OFFERED, THERE IS NO EXCUSE FOR NOT KNOWING THE CONDITION OF AN ITEM.  IF THERE IS A DISCREPANCY THEN IT NEEDS TO BE DISCUSSED PRIOR TO BEING TAKEN OFF PREMISES. title: Online March 5th Luxury & Home Furnishings Auction
description:
READ BELOW. Important Auction Information

1.) Register to bid. At the top of your screen you will see a button labeled "Login/New Bidder". You will need to click this and fill out the basic information to create your new account or login to your existing account. If you have bid with us in the past and have forgotten your Username or Password, you can reset them from there. There is no charge to register and bid, you only pay if you win an item. The credit card on file will be validated by our software company. This means they will put a "hold" on your credit card for $1 and then reverse that charge within 24 hours. This ensures the card is valid and that you are bidding against only other valid bidders.

2.) Preview the entire catalog early and often. New items are added to the catalog as they come in to the warehouse and can sometimes fall on different pages depending on where they are placed in the building. It isn't unusual for new items to be added just minutes before the auction begins.

3.) Pick the items you would like to bid on and PRE-BID with realistic numbers. We can not stress this enough. Items can be lost in the final seconds due to last second bids, Internet outages, power or site failures. The computer will bid on your behalf up to your maximum amount if some else places a bid.
EXAMPLE A.) You place a bid of $100 for an item. Someone else bids $50 and nobody else bids, you win your item for $55.
EXAMPLE B.) You place a bid of $100 for an item. Someone else bids $100 and nobody else bids, you will still win your item at $100 because it is the first bid.
EXAMPLE C.) You place a bid of $100 for an item. Someone else bids $110 in the final seconds. Bidding extends 2 minutes and you will only have 2 minutes total to bid again.

4.) PLEASE READ the terms and conditions. All bidders are bound by the same terms and conditions for this auction included payment options, pickup times and other things important to the auction. Most questions will be answered on our website, but if you have one that isn't you can click the "Feedback" button at the bottom of your screen and we will help you.

5.) Have fun and save money! Interstate Auction & Realty has revolutionized the way new furniture is being sold with our Internet Platform. Do not under estimate how much money you can save. We have buyers every week from several hundred miles away who cannot believe the savings!

SOME OF THE ITEMS TO BE AUCTIONED OFF INCLUDE AN ENITRE SOLEM BREAD INDUSTRIAL PRODUCTION LINE, INCLUDING DOUGH CUTTER, DOUGH ROLLER, DOUGH FORMING AND BAKING, SOLEM 200 QUART SPIRAL MIXER, EMPIRE 200 QUART SPIRAL MIXER, LVO SHEETER/ MOLDER, EXCELLENT BAGEL EQUIPMENT, OLIVER BAGGING MACHINE, REVENT 8 RACK PROOFER, ABS 4 DECK BREAD OVEN AND MUCH MORE !!

HERE IS A MORE COMPLETE DESCRIPTION OF THE EQUIPMENT :

Complete Bread Make-Up Line – Divider, Rounder, Intermediate Proofer & Moulder

Professional commercial bread make-up line designed for gentle dough handling and consistent production quality. The volumetric double piston divider divides without pressing, allowing even the most sensitive doughs to be processed as if divided by hand. Stainless-steel, Teflon-coated food-contact parts ensure hygiene and easy cleaning. Output capacity is 20–40 pieces per minute with manual or automatic weight adjustment and automatic lubrication system.

The rounder features fixed spiral channels, silent operation, flouring system, and electronic speed control, maintaining dough quality with an air-blowing system suitable for varying production environments. Handles dough weights up to 800 g.

The intermediate proofer provides controlled resting for dough coming from the divider or rounder, with removable hygienic pockets, dough counting system, and direct electrical connection to the full line.

The moulder shapes dough up to 300mm length, with adjustable stainless rollers, 200–800 g weight range, and capacity of up to 2,400 pieces per hour.
This line was only used for three months extra brand New parts are available with this production line. 


Ideal for industrial and semi-industrial bakeries

title: Estate of Patricia Baker- Springfield IL
description:
Estate of Patricia Baker- Springfield IL
Featuring: 197 Lots featuring: Furniture, Household, & More
Address: 2304 Sutherland Rd., Springfield IL 62707

Auction Ends: Thursday March 5th at 7:00 PM CST (Soft Close)

In-Person Preview: Tuesday March 3rd 4pm - 6pm
2304 Sutherland Rd., Springfield IL 62707

Item Pick-Up: Monday March 9th, 3:00pm - 6:00pm
Pick-up location: 2304 Sutherland Rd., Springfield IL 62707

Pickup appointments must be scheduled via Shipping Saint 
(email/text link provided after auction ends).

Ben Ladage Auctions - Auburn, IL
Office: 217-652-0924
View full catalog and bid at: www.benladage.com
Please read the complete Terms & Conditions before placing any bids.
________________________________________
BUYER'S PREMIUM & PAYMENT:

A 15% buyer's premium will be added to each item.
We offer a 3% discount for cash, check, or non-credit card payment - you must notify us before the auction closes to receive this discount.

Accepted payment forms: Visa, Mastercard, Discover, debit card, cash, or approved checks.
No checks accepted without prior approval or established business history. Checks with bank letter of credit may be accepted-please call our office in advance.

Invoices for items will be billed to your registered credit card immediately following the auction. If shipping is selected, you will receive a separate invoice once shipping costs are calculated.
________________________________________
FOLLOWING THE AUCTION:

Winning bidders will receive both an email and text message with the option to select pickup or shipping.
o If pickup is selected, you'll receive full pickup instructions, including date, time, and location based on the information you provided during registration.
o If shipping is selected, your items will be securely packed by our in-house shipping department. Once packaging is complete, you will receive another email and text message with the total shipping cost and handling fee.
________________________________________
SHIPPING:

Shipping is available on most items and provided in-house by Ben Ladage Auctions using Shipping Saint. After the auction:
"	You will receive an email and text to choose either shipping or pick-up.
"	If shipping is selected, items will be packaged, and you will receive a second notification with shipping and handling costs.
"	We use USPS, UPS, and FedEx and always select the most cost-effective method.
"	Please allow up to 15 business days for items to ship after the pickup date, although most packages go out within 5 business days.
We do not ship large or heavy items unless pre-approved prior to auction closing. We do not accept outside shipping labels or provide shipping quotes. Shipping costs are the responsibility of the buyer and are non-refundable.
________________________________________
ITEM DESCRIPTIONS, INSPECTIONS, & DISCLAIMERS:

All items are sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" with no warranties, guarantees, refunds, or returns of any kind. Descriptions including condition, age, origin, or rarity are offered as opinion only. The bidder assumes all responsibility for determining value and authenticity.
Preview and inspection are strongly encouraged. The auctioneer is not responsible for errors in descriptions or missed bids (online, absentee, phone, or otherwise). All announcements on auction day take precedence over any prior marketing.
________________________________________
PICKUP POLICY - READ CAREFULLY:

All items must be picked up on the scheduled date and time. If you cannot make the scheduled pickup, please do not bid.
Items not picked up are forfeited and become property of Ben Ladage Auctions unless other arrangements have been made in advance.
We understand life happens and will try to work with you-but communication is key. If we have to retrieve your items from the auction site and transport them back to Auburn, a minimum fee of $25 per item will be charged. This fee helps cover time, labor, and tracking logistics.
________________________________________
ABSENTEE BIDDING & TECHNICAL INFORMATION:

Don't want to watch the auction live? The best strategy is to leave your maximum bid ahead of time. Our system will bid incrementally on your behalf.
This is a soft close auction. If a bid is placed in the last 3 minutes of a lot's closing, that lot will automatically extend by 3 minutes. All lots close in staggered intervals.
If you're unsure how to bid online, contact our office before the final day-we're happy to help! Absentee bids are accepted and will include the 15% buyer's premium.
________________________________________

Sellers: Robert Strocher -Executor

We appreciate your bids and support-thank you for participating in this online auction!

PAYMENT

IN THE EVENT A CREDIT CARD DOESN'T GO THROUGH THE NIGHT OF AUCTION THERE WILL BE A LINK SENT OVER TO PAY VIA ZELLE.  IF THAT INVOICE IS NOT PAID THE NIGHT OF.  THERE WILL BE A $10 INCONVENIENCE FEE ADDED.  IF THE BALANCE IS NOT PAID FOR BIDDING PRIVILEGES WILL BE SUSPENDED IN HIBID.   

IF YOU ARE BIDDING ON GOLD OR POTENTIALLY OTHER EXPENSIVE ITEMS .  I WILL REQUIRE A ZELLE OR CASH PAYMENT ALWAYS IF YOU ARE A NEW BIDDER OR OUT OF STATE.   AND POSSIBLY IF YOU ARE LOCAL.   IT WILL BE AT THE AUCTIONEERS DISCRETION IF I CHOOSE NOT TO.   ESTABLISHED BIDDERS WILL NOT HAVE TO WORRY ABOUT THIS. 

WE DO NOT TAKE OUT OF STATE TAX PERMITS. AND WE CHECK VALIDITY OF IN STATE TAX PERMITS PRIOR TO REMOVING SALES TAX.

RECEIPT OF ITEMS AND SIGNING OF INVOICE.

MOST AUCTIONS HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  IN HOME ESTATES DO NOT HAVE THIS ABILITY AND THEREFORE YOU ARE BIDDING ON AN ITEM BASED ON THE PICTURES AND DESCRIPTION GIVEN KNOWING THAT AN ITEM IF NOT LISTED AS NEW MAY HAVE WEAR, TEAR, OR OTHER POSSIBLE DEFECTS.    THIS MEANS UNLESS I MAKE A MISTAKE SAY I LIST A TV AS A MICROWAVE, A TWIN BED AS A FULL OR SIMILAR OBVIOUS MISTAKE THEN ALL ITEMS ARE AS IS WHERE IS WITH NO WARRANTY EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.   

IF IT IS AT THE AUCTION HOUSE.   A PREVIEW IS ALWAYS OFFERED, THERE IS NO EXCUSE FOR NOT KNOWING THE CONDITION OF AN ITEM.  IF THERE IS A DISCREPANCY THEN IT NEEDS TO BE DISCUSSED PRIOR TO BEING TAKEN OFF PREMISES.   ONCE THE INVOICE IS SIGNED YOU ARE AGREEING THAT THE ITEM IS ACCEPTABLE IN ITS DESCRIPTION AND FORM AND NO FURTHER DISCUSSION WILL OCCUR ON THE MATTER.   ULTIMATELY I AM THE FINAL SAY ON THE MATTER AND RESERVE THAT RIGHT.

IF YOU ARE BIDDING AND REQUIRE SHIPPING AND YOU DO NOT HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  YOU ARE BIDDING ON THE ITEM IN A BIT OF A BLIND FORMAT.  YOU ARE ACCEPTING THE ITEM BASED SOLELY ON THE PICTURES PROVIDED AND DESCRIPTION.  AGAIN IN THIS CASE YOU ARE RECOGNIZING THAT UNLESS AN ITEM IS DESCRIBED AS NEW THEN THERE MAY BE INTERNAL EXTERNAL FLAWS AND SIGNS OF USAGE AND YOU ARE BIDDING ON THESE ITEMS AS IS WHERE IS NO WARRANTIES ARE EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.

title: FRI WEEKLY #5 COINS ART PARTIAL
description:
PARTIAL: PREVIEW IS M,T,W,THU FRI 9-5 CLOSED 12-1 PU IS SAT & SUN 10-5 CLOSED 12-1

WE SHIP IN HOUSE.  THIS MEANS BETTER RATES, FASTER TURN OVER FOR SHIPPING.  YOU WILL RECEIVE A TEXT MESSAGE OR EMAIL POST AUCTION.  IF YOU NEED SHIPPING FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS.

PLEASE PICKUP ITEMS BY END OF DAY SUNDAY 5PM FOLLOWING THE AUCTION.  

IF YOU CAN NOT THERE ARE OPTIONS FOR SMALLER ITEMS. FURNITURE WILL NOT BE ALLOWED FOR LATE PICKUP AS WE HAVE LIMITED SPACE.

1. EMAIL AT TYLERGRACEAUCTIONS@GMAIL.COM AND ASK FOR A LATE PICKUP. 

2. REQUEST SHIPPING THROUGH LINKS PROVIDED

3. CONTACT A MOVER OF YOURS OR A RECOMMENDED MOVER AT THE BOTTOM OF YOUR INVOICE PAGE.

IF YOU DO NONE OF THESE AND JUST ABANDON YOUR ITEMS THEY WILL BE RELISTED AND YOUR ITEMS AND MONIES WILL BE FORFEITED.

SHIPPING 

THERE WILL BE A LINK PROVIDED IN THE EMAIL AND OR TEXT MESSAGES FOLLOW THE INSTRUCTIONS PROVIDED.

AFTER 48 HOURS IF NO RESPONSE WE WILL JUST SHIP USING THE CREDIT CARD ON FILE, BUT THIS SLOWS THE PROCESS DOWN, YOU DONT GET TEXT MESSAGES WITH TRACKING UPDATES BECAUSE YOU DIDNT CLICK THE LINK SENT TO YOU.   BUT WE CAN NOT HOLD ONTO ITEMS WAITING ON THAT PROCESS.   

IF NO CONTACT HAS BEEN MADE AND THE PAYMENT DOESNT GO THROUGH FOR SHIPPING, NORMAL ABANDONMENT TIMES AND FORFEITURE POLICIES WILL APPLY.

PAYMENT

I REQUIRE A GOOD CREDIT CARD ON FILE TO BID THAT CAN BE CHARGED THE NIGHT OF AUCTION.   IN THE EVENT A CREDIT CARD DOESN'T GO THROUGH THE NIGHT OF AUCTION THERE WILL BE A LINK SENT OVER TO PAY VIA ZELLE.  IF THAT INVOICE IS NOT PAID THE NIGHT OF.  THERE WILL BE A $10 INCONVENIENCE FEE ADDED.  IF THE BALANCE IS NOT PAID FOR BIDDING PRIVILEGES WILL BE SUSPENDED IN HIBID.   

IF YOU ARE BIDDING ON GOLD OR POTENTIALLY OTHER EXPENSIVE ITEMS .  I WILL REQUIRE A ZELLE OR CASH PAYMENT ALWAYS IF YOU ARE A NEW BIDDER OR OUT OF STATE.   AND POSSIBLY IF YOU ARE LOCAL.   IT WILL BE AT THE AUCTIONEERS DISCRETION IF I CHOOSE NOT TO.   ESTABLISHED BIDDERS WILL NOT HAVE TO WORRY ABOUT THIS. 

WE DO NOT TAKE OUT OF STATE TAX PERMITS. AND WE CHECK VALIDITY OF IN STATE TAX PERMITS PRIOR TO REMOVING SALES TAX.

RECEIPT OF ITEMS AND SIGNING OF INVOICE.

MOST AUCTIONS HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  IN HOME ESTATES DO NOT HAVE THIS ABILITY AND THEREFORE YOU ARE BIDDING ON AN ITEM BASED ON THE PICTURES AND DESCRIPTION GIVEN KNOWING THAT AN ITEM IF NOT LISTED AS NEW MAY HAVE WEAR, TEAR, OR OTHER POSSIBLE DEFECTS.    THIS MEANS UNLESS I MAKE A MISTAKE SAY I LIST A TV AS A MICROWAVE, A TWIN BED AS A FULL OR SIMILAR OBVIOUS MISTAKE THEN ALL ITEMS ARE AS IS WHERE IS WITH NO WARRANTY EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.   

IF IT IS AT THE AUCTION HOUSE.   A PREVIEW IS ALWAYS OFFERED, THERE IS NO EXCUSE FOR NOT KNOWING THE CONDITION OF AN ITEM.  IF THERE IS A DISCREPANCY THEN IT NEEDS TO BE DISCUSSED PRIOR TO BEING TAKEN OFF PREMISES.   ONCE THE INVOICE IS SIGNED YOU ARE AGREEING THAT THE ITEM IS ACCEPTABLE IN ITS DESCRIPTION AND FORM AND NO FURTHER DISCUSSION WILL OCCUR ON THE MATTER.   ULTIMATELY I AM THE FINAL SAY ON THE MATTER AND RESERVE THAT RIGHT.

IF YOU ARE BIDDING AND REQUIRE SHIPPING AND YOU DO NOT HAVE THE ABILITY TO PREVIEW.  YOU ARE BIDDING ON THE ITEM IN A BIT OF A BLIND FORMAT.  YOU ARE ACCEPTING THE ITEM BASED SOLELY ON THE PICTURES PROVIDED AND DESCRIPTION.  AGAIN IN THIS CASE YOU ARE RECOGNIZING THAT UNLESS AN ITEM IS DESCRIBED AS NEW THEN THERE MAY BE INTERNAL EXTERNAL FLAWS AND SIGNS OF USAGE AND YOU ARE BIDDING ON THESE ITEMS AS IS WHERE IS NO WARRANTIES ARE EXPRESSED OR IMPLIED.

I RESERVE THE RIGHT TO BE FINAL AUTHORITY ON ANY DISCREPANCY OR ISSUE THAT MAY COME ABOUT IN THE COURSE OF THE AUCTION PROCESS.

title: 3/7/26 March Military & Sportsman Auction
description:
March Military/Sportsman Auction - Online Only

Selling without reserves to the highest bidder, regardless of price!

Auction Highlights:This online-only auction includes over 2000 lots, featuring a wide range of items for the avid outdoorsman and collector: 

** Featuring Militaria* Shotguns * Rifles * Pistols * Revolvers; Airguns; Knives & Swords; Decoys; Ammo; Scopes & Optics;  Mounts; Generators; Fishing Equipment; Archery And More!

Auction Details:
Auction Ending: Saturday, March 7, 2026 starting at 9:00 AM (Eastern Standard Time).

Staggered Closing: Items will close every 20 seconds. If a bid is placed within the last 3 minutes of an item's closing, the bidding time will automatically extend by an additional 2 minutes.

LIVE CATALOG: You can follow along with the auction in real-time by clicking the LIVE CATALOG button on the day of the auction.

In-Person Preview: Friday, Fri. March 6, 2026 from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM at Hessney Auction Center, 2741 Rt 14 N, Geneva, NY 14456.
Preview any items before you bid.Ask questions or request additional photos if needed.

Payment Terms:
Payment Methods:Credit Cards: Your registered credit card will be charged after the auction ends (typically Saturday evening or Sunday morning).
NOTE:Cash, NYS Check, Certified Check, or Wire Transfer: These methods are accepted, but you must request to pay this way by 4:00 PM on Friday before the auction ends.

Buyer’s Premium: A 13% buyer’s premium will be added to your winning bid, regardless of payment type.
Sales Tax: NYS Buyers: A 7.5% New York State sales tax will be applied.If you are tax-exempt, please submit your resale certificate prior to the auction closing. Once your payment is processed, we cannot refund sales tax.

Invoices: Invoices will be emailed to winning bidders by Sunday afternoon. If you do not receive your invoice, please check your SPAM folder.

Firearm & Ammo Purchases - New Regulations:

NYS Firearm Background Check: Effective September 13, 2023, a background check is required for all firearm, rifle, shotgun, muzzle loaders, and ammunition purchases in New York State.$9.00 fee per firearm transaction $2.50 fee per ammunition transaction. Total fee for a firearm and ammunition on the same invoice will be $11.50.
NYS NICS Fee:These fees will be added as a line item to your invoice and charged when we run your credit card.
Out-of-state buyers picking up firearms or ammo must pay these fees in cash.

Shipping Ammunition (NYS Buyers): Ammunition can be shipped only to an FFL.

Out-of-state buyers: Ammunition will be shipped via UPS (due to USPS restrictions), and there will be a $5.00 handling fee per package.

Pickup Information:Pickup Dates & Times:Please adhere to the following schedule for item pickup: THE WEEK FOLLOWING AUCTION CLOSING
Monday: 9:30 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 12:00 PM - 7:00 PM; Wednesday: 9:30 AM - 5:00 PM, Friday 9:30am-5pm

Note: Our office is closed on Thursdays and Sundays.

_____________________________________________________________________
*NEW- Delivery *             
* This is for Buyers within a 300 mile radius of Hessney Auction.              
* This IS NOT FOR FIREARMS OR AMMUNITION. You can select delivery for a cost for any accessory that does not require State or Federal paperwork. (Example: Walmart Delivery / Door Dash) When selecting this option the system will determine weather this is a small (front seat), medium (back seat), large (SUV) or Huge (truck) item. Items will have a set pick up date for the delivery driver at Hessney Auction, which is determined after each auction. Delivery drivers are subcontracts and not employees of Hessney Auction Co. Any questions or for more information contact us to determine if this is a good option for you!
___________________________________________________________________________

Firearm Purchases:Firearm purchases require an appointment. A link to schedule an appointment will be sent with your invoice.If you live in a county that requires special paperwork (e.g., Monroe County), you must request the required pistol paperwork by email at least 24 hours before your appointment.

Important Notes for Firearm Buyers:Firearms must be picked up by the registered purchaser (no third-party pickups).If purchasing a handgun, contact your county for instructions on adding it to your pistol permit.
Monroe County Buyers: Extra paperwork is required. Email your request at least 24 hours in advance to ensure we can assist you.

Ammo & Accessories:Ammo and accessories can be picked up during scheduled pickup times without an appointment.

Loading Items:Bring assistance if you are purchasing large or heavy items such as generators, gun safes, or other bulky items.Special equipment may be necessary for safe handling (e.g., for gun safes).

Shipping Information:
Shipping Options:After the auction ends, you will be contacted via Shipping Saint to choose between "Shipping" or "Pickup".Once your items are in the shipping area, you will receive an update with the cost of shipping and tracking information.All shipping costs are the responsibility of the buyer.

Handling Fees: Each package will have a $5 to $10 handling fee depending on size. USPS Priority Mail boxes incur a $5 flat handling fee.

Shipping Process:Please allow up to 2 weeks for shipping. We have a small staff but will make every effort to ship items promptly.

Firearm shipments will begin on Thursday following the auction.We do not ship internationally (except to Canada), and we cannot ship primers, powder, or mounts.

Firearm Shipping: 
FFL Requirements: For firearm shipping, please have your FFL email us a copy of their license along with the buyers name. If you are a C&R license holder, please provide your C&R license.A $10 handling fee will be applied to each firearm shipment. All shipping costs, including postage and insurance, are the buyer’s responsibility.
Ammunition Shipping:NYS residents: Ammunition must be shipped to an FFL.For out-of-state buyers, we will follow federal shipping regulations, and ammunition will be shipped via UPS.A $5 handling fee will apply per ammunition shipment.

Important Legal Information:
NYS Background Check (NY NICS):All firearm purchases require a NY NICS background check unless the buyer has a Federal Firearms License (FFL).Handguns require a NYS Pistol Permit (unless the buyer has a Federal Firearms License).Some firearms may not be NYS compliant. Please read item descriptions carefully before bidding.
SEMI AUTO RIFLE PERMITS ARE REQUIRED for NYS Semi Auto Rifle purchases.

Additional Notes:No Returns: All items are sold as-is, with no returns accepted. Please review descriptions and ask questions before bidding.
Buyer's Responsibility: It is the buyer’s responsibility to follow all local, state, and federal laws related to the purchase, possession, and transfer of firearms and ammunition.

Please refer to "Terms and Conditions" of this auction for any further information.

title: Klausmeier Farms Retirement Auction
description:
Wachter Auctions & Appraisals is honored to present the contents of the Klausmeier Farms Retirement Auction, located at 4014 Perry Hall Rd. Klausmeier Farms was established in 1915, in Perry Hall, Maryland. The Klausmeier family is closing a chapter in their lives and will be auctioning most of their farming equipment. This past fall was the last harvest that the family's equipment was being used. Having owned and operated an automotive business for years, their equipment has been very well maintained.  

The auction format for this sale is Simulcast and Live only. A Simulcast Auction is an auction where the items sell both live in person while simultaneously selling through an online platform. All of the larger equipment will be offered through the simulcast portion of the auction. To see the entire auction, please be here in person to have the opportunity to bid on items in the Live, In-Person Only portion. The in-person only portion will include hand tools, mechanical tools, equipment parts and much more. Auction set up is continuing and more items for the live in-person only portion will be added.  To see items available IN PERSON ONLY view: www.WachterAuctions.com/Klausmeier

All successful buyers must make arrangements to remove items from the farm's location during the pick-up days scheduled.  There will be NO shipping available from Wachter Auctions for this auction.  If a buyer wishes to have something shipped to them, the buyer is responsible to hire a third party shipper that can remove remove the item from the premises with a paid receipt.

Buyers of the Grain Bins will be granted additional time to remove them from the premises, up to early summer, please talk to a Wachter Auction & Appraisal associate prior to bidding to ensure you have enough time to remove the grain bins. Items may be added, edited or removed in the catalog at any time during the auction process, before the hammer falls.

title: March 7th Secured Creditor & Repo Auction
description:
What to Expect at Our Secured Creditor & Repo Auction

Our Secured Creditor & Repo Auction features vehicles and assets that have been repossessed, recovered, or released by financial institutions, lenders, and secured creditors. These auctions offer buyers the opportunity to purchase inventory directly from the source, often at competitive pricing.

INVENTORY YOU'LL SEE
Expect a wide mix of vehicles and equipment, including: Cars, trucks, SUVs, vans, Fleet and commercial vehicles, Bank repossessions, Specialty or work vehicles (varies by sale)

Inventory changes by the sale and may include everything from ready-to-drive units to project or non-running vehicles.

TITLES & PAPERWORK
Titles and documents are provided according to secured creditor guidelines. Some vehicles may require additional processing time for paperwork. Repo-related disclosures will be announced when applicable

AUCTION DAY EXPERIENCE
Fast-paced, professional auction environment. Knowledgeable staff available to assist. On-site registration required prior to bidding. Payment terms and removal deadlines apply.

WHO SHOULD ATTEND?
Dealers and resellers, Small businesses and fleets, Contractors and trades, Value-driven buyers looking for opportunity inventory. If you’re looking for authentic repossessions, lender inventory, and real auction value, our Secured Creditor & Repo Auction delivers auction after auction.

Check the sale listing regularly—inventory is always changing!

title: Epic Winter Sporting Auction - Guns, Knives, Lures, Relics
description:
Epic Auctions is proud to present a diverse 456-lot firearms and sporting collectibles auction featuring an exceptional mix of military arms, collector-grade rifles and handguns, modern sporting firearms, edged weapons, vintage fishing tackle, ammunition, and accessories. This is a well-rounded offering that appeals to serious collectors, shooters, outdoorsmen, and enthusiasts alike.

Highlights include a Colt New Service Revolver in .45 Colt, a Winchester M1 Garand in .30-06, Inland and Winchester M1 Carbines, and a Saginaw Gear M1 Carbine from WWII production. Additional military and surplus standouts include a Remington Model 1903-A3, a French MAS 1949-56 semi-automatic rifle, and a 1954 Russian SKS. The handgun selection features a Smith & Wesson Model 41 target pistol, a Colt .357 Magnum revolver, and a St. Etienne Model 1892 revolver, offering both collector interest and shooting appeal.

Sporting firearm enthusiasts will appreciate the strong lineup of Browning rifles, including FN Safari bolt-action models and BLR Lightning lever-action rifles in popular calibers, along with a Colt Match Target HBAR .223 rifle. The auction also includes a variety of bolt-action hunting rifles, lever-actions, rimfires, and classic single-shot and side-by-side shotguns, creating depth across multiple categories.

Beyond firearms, this auction features nearly 50 hand-forged hunting and skinning knives crafted with traditional techniques and quality materials, as well as more than 30 lots of vintage fishing lures from respected makers such as Shakespeare, Pflueger, Creek Chub, Heddon, and Canadian manufacturers. Additional offerings include Thompson/Center Contender barrels, scopes, ammunition, and other sporting accessories.

There is a 15% Buyer's Premium plus a 4% credit card fee. (Cash preferred...)

title: CGC, Comics & Collectibles! Estate Auction Part 3
description:
This fantastic auction features a massive pop-culture and comic book collection that includes the last part of a local Detroit area collectible store owner as well as a few other consignors that are offering items from the following categories: Horror, punk, metal, anime, Star Wars, Simpsons, and vintage toy lots alongside Silver, Bronze, and Modern Age key issue comics and CGC/CBCS graded books. 
Highlights include classic horror VHS and DVD lots, Maximum Rock'n'roll and Flipside punk zines, vintage band shirts, Metropolis and James Owen signed prints, Atari 2600 games, Star Wars vintage action figures and vehicles, G.I. Joe vintage figures, Simpsons World of Springfield figure sets, and large mixed Marvel/DC comic long and short box lots perfect for dealers and collectors.   Comic collectors will find major keys such as Amazing Spider-Man #129 (1st Punisher), Amazing Spider-Man #135 (2nd Punisher), Amazing Spider-Man #300 (1st Venom), Amazing Spider-Man #361 (1st Carnage), X-Men #94 (2nd new team), All-Star Comics #58 (1st Power Girl), New Mutants #98 (1st Deadpool), Harbinger #1 and #0, X-Men Annual #14 (early Gambit), and multiple copies of Simpsons Comics and Stories #1 and X-Men #1 (1991) by Jim Lee in both raw and CGC 9.6/9.8 grades. This sale also includes runs and near-runs like Ms. Marvel (2006) #1–50, Godzilla (1977) Marvel, Conan, Red Sonja, She-Hulk, Red Hulk, World War Hulk/Planet Hulk tie-ins, KISS, Power Girl, Teen Titans (1st Nightwing/Vigilante), and more. 

Highlights: 
197	X-Men #99 + #100
198	Amazing Spider-Man #129/1st Punisher
198a	G.I. Joe Vintage Snake Eyes + Storm Shadow
199	Amazing Spider-Man #300 CGC 9.4 1st Venom 
52a	Conan #0-50 Full Run w/Rare Nude Variant #24
10	Melvins Rare Signed Vinyl Newsletter Mixed Merch
94	Eek The Cat Tiny Toons Adventures Futurama Lot
112	Misfits Samhain Vinyl Collectibles Posters Lot
160	Maximum RocknRoll Zine Lot #58-99
164	Toxic Crusader Action Figure Lot w/Accessories
194	Horror Zombies Crypt Keepers More VHS Lot
196	WWF Action Figure Mag Lot Steiner Bulldogs Hasbro
200	Jeffrey Dahmer 90s Parody Shirt + Mag Lot

For local bidders, lots from this auction may be picked up and invoices can be paid starting at 12pm noon on the Monday following the auction's close. For everyone else, shipping costs will be assessed and invoices will start becoming available later that day. Pick-up is available Monday through Friday, 9 AM to 5 PM, at our shipping & receiving entrance.
Please note we have moved locations so make sure you are picking up at the Farmington Hills location!

title: Dear Diamnds And Jewelry Auction Ends 03/07/2026
description:
READ BELOW - Important Auction Information

HOW DOES THIS WORK? It's simple. Dear Diamonds and Jewelry is an online public auction company specializing in jewelry, antique coins and other rarities. These items are offered for sale to the public on our website for a fixed period of time with a defined closing date and time. To register you simply sign up for an account on our secure website. Your information is validated and then you may begin placing your bids!

HOW DO I REGISTER? At the top of your screen, click "Login/New Bidder." If you are a new bidder, click the black box. This will prompt you to enter your email address. You will be emailed a link to validate your email, and then you can finish your registration. It's fast, easy and secure. The whole process takes about 2 minutes. Your credit card will be validated by and in doing so, a temporary hold of $1 will be placed on your account to ensure the credit card is valid, and that only legitimate bidders are participating. This process is exactly like paying for gas at the pump! The charge will fall off of your account in 24-48 hours.

HOW DOES THE AUCTION WORK? The auction is open for bidding as soon as it goes "LIVE" online. Bidding is super easy, at any time you can begin placing your bids and may enter a maximum bid.

title: Chevy Silverado, Gold, Furniture, Rare Books, & Sports Cards
description:
Don't miss this incredible multi category auction packed with over 600 lots of unique finds! Whether you collect, restore, decorate, or just love great deals-there's something here for everyone.

Featuring a 2003 Chevy Silverado 2500 HD (67,212 miles) in great condition from original owner, and garage kept. 

Jewelry lovers will find 14k gold rings, diamond pieces, Black Hills Gold, sterling opal and moss agate rings, vintage watches, costume jewelry lots, and several filled jewelry boxes. 

The book and ephemera selections include 'Alice in Wonderland & Through...' By Lewis Carroll, 'Sinking of the Titanic' First Ed. By L.H. Walter, 'The Keeper of Bees' First Ed. By Gene S. Porter, 'Treasure Island' By Robert Louis Stevenson, 'Robinson Crusoe' By Daniel DeFoe, children's books from the early 1900s, 1880s-1940s literature, and many other first editions. 

Sports collectors will be thrilled with 60+ lots featuring Willie Mays, Mickey Mantle, Bart Starr rookies, Harmon Killebrew, Minnie Minoso, Bo Jackson, Nolan Ryan, unopened packs, miscut/error cards, Notre Dame pennants, postcards, and decades of Topps, Fleer, Donruss and Skybox. 

Over 70 lots of beautiful furniture such as an Ethan Allen dining set, antique dressers, cabinets, tables, mirrors, sewing machine cabinets and classic chairs. Art and décor lovers will find paintings, stained glass windows, sculptures, prints, uranium glass, swanky swigs, depression glass, Van Briggle pottery, and lamps. 

Of course there is more! Snapper riding mower, and a full lineup of tools including an arc welder, drill press, pressure washer and more. Philco tube radio console, Zenith record console, Underwood typewriter, and a massive selection of toys, textiles, and linens. 

This sale is packed with treasures for collectors, vintage lovers, decorators, resellers and bargain hunters-you won't want to miss it!

This auction begins closing Saturday, March 7, 2026 at 7:00pm EST, with pickup on Sunday, March 8, 2026, from 11:00AM-3:00PM EST at Crown Event Center in New Carlisle, IN. 

Tricia Wainscott, Licensed Auctioneer & Realtor
574-329-1204
Lic#AU11300117

OUR Auctions may be "RESERVE AUCTIONS".  We have many items that due to the price of the item, has a "Reserve" on the item.  That means as an example, an item can't be sold for much less than "Melt Price" of gold or silver or the prevailing price of an item.  If the Melt Price of a silver dollar is $48.00e can't sell it for $10.00, we could sell it to a refrinery for close to melt price. A Buywer Fee of 18% and a 3.5 Credit Card Fee will be added.  A coin for a retail price of $800.00 certaintly can't be sold for $25.00.  If we sold items at less than we paid for them we wouldn't be in business very long.  I think everyone can understand that.  We don't or have anyone eles "Shill, or bid up up our Auctions!

TERMS OF OUR AUCTION
 
DESCRIPTION 
 
We provide Title, Pictures and Description for items as best we can.
We sell all Items as is, where is.

title: Lewis & Maese March 8th, 2026 Antiques & Art Auction

Online Estate Auction
Sunday, March 8th at 11:00 AM
500 Lots of Fine Art, Jewelry, Antiques & Designer Furnishings
Get ready for one of our most diverse and exciting estate auctions of the season.

Lewis & Maese Antiques & Auctions is proud to present 500 carefully curated lots featuring fine jewelry, designer furniture, original artwork, bronze sculpture, Persian rugs, and one of a kind decorative pieces sourced from distinguished estates and private collections. Whether you are a collector, interior designer, or simply love discovering unique pieces with history and character, this sale has something for every taste and budget. From museum quality modern design to timeless 19th century antiques, every lot sells to the highest bidder.

Bid online from anywhere and have your treasures shipped directly to your door.

title: MARCH MADNESS 2026 ANTIQUE AUCTION
description: Welcome to our March Madness 2026 Antique Auction. If you are looking for ready items for Round Top, collectibles for our personal collection or wanting to just try your luck on winning to gift someone special, you don't want to miss this auction. title: Sokoloski Estate Auction of Knoxville, TN
description:
Welcome to the Sokoloski Estate, brought to you by Chrissy and Maggie G. of JD's Realty & Auction!

Explore a unique curation of collectibles, furniture, jewelry, and more at this East Knoxville estate!

Highlights include:
-Sterling silver jewelry pieces
-Tiffany-style lamps
-Uranium and Amberina glass pieces
-Art glass
-Framed artwork
-Fashion jewelry and jewelry-making collection
-Much more!

**Important Info** 
This estate is located in an apartment complex. The apartment is on the main level, but parking may be limited earlier in the morning and later in the day. We ask that you use courtesy when driving through and parking, especially if you are bringing a larger vehicle or trailer.

The apartment number will only be shared after the auction by email.

*Please note that this is a dog and cat friendly home.

IMPORTANT PICK UP INFO

-Please bring any help, dollies, and tools necessary to move and disassemble furniture and heavy items. **We are unable to help you move heavy furniture.**

-Items must be picked up during allotted pickup times. If you are unable to pick up in that time frame, please do not bid. We only have access to the estate during the published times.

-If you wish to pick up your items from our shipper, you will be assessed a surcharge. This is not available on all items.

For questions concerning items in the auction, please call or text Maggie G. at 865-603-4388 or Chrissy at 248-894-4634. We do recommend scheduling a time to preview as to better judge items for yourself, especially if you have multiple questions.

Any questions about shipping should be directed to our third party shipper (see below) Items for shipping will not be taken to shipper until after pickup is over. Please give the shipper time to assess your items and they will contact you with an estimate by email. Liberty Pack & Ship 134 S. Main St. Clinton, TN 37716 865-207-0753 or info@libertypackandship.com

Thank you!

Principal Auctioneer: Jason Deel TAL 6826

title: Sunday - March 8th @ 1 PM
description:
Sunday - March 8th @1 PM   
Coins - Silver - Gold  - Jewelry - Antiques - Collectibles  
-----------------------------------------------------------------
By signing up for our auction you agree to the following: 
NOTE: Any sign up with negative feedback will not be approved to bid.  Any bidder that is new to our auctions (with zero feedback) and you win bids on gold - silver - precious metals or have an invoice total over $250 on any wins and will need shipping - you will be asked to send a photo of your driver's license and a copy of the credit card that you used to sign up (name and address on driver's license and the name on the credit card must match the sign up information). WE WILL NOT RISK SHIPPING ITEMS WITHOUT THIS INFORMATION. This is called - know your bidder per Fraudulant Claims Services.  DO NOT SEND THIS INFORMATION UNTIL WE ASK FOR IT.   If you are New to the auction and picking up in person - if you plan on using a credit card - be prepared to show your Driver's License and it must match the name on the credit card being used. CASH or CARD ONLY on in person pickup - NO CHECKS!!!  PLEASE NOTE:  Coins - gold - silver = CASH ONLY!!!
THIS IS THE WAY WE DO THINGS: ALL INVOICES HAVE A 15% BUYERS PREMIUM. You will receive your UNPAID invoice within 15 minutes of the close of the auction (so you know which items you have won). NOTE: YOU CANNOT PAY THIS INVOICE...READ:  If you choose to pickup, you can pay with CASH - (NO CHECKS) OR you can use your card in person with an added 3.5% card fee.  Pick up will be Wednesday - 03/11/2026 from 4:00 PM - 6 PM.   DO NOT BID if you cannot make the pickup date and time. PLEASE BE a responsible bidder and follow the directions herein. Need Shipping? Read below carefully-- OUT OF STATE invoices - We will run your card on file with HIBID within 1 hour of the auction end for the invoice total only!!  PLEASE NOTE: WE DO NOT HAVE ACCESS TO YOUR CARD ON FILE FOR THE SHIPPING AND HANDLING FEES -  READ ON:  SPEED WILL BE DEPENDANT ON THE NUMBER OF WINNERS.  We assume all out of state winners will need shipping UNLESS you text your name and the words PICK UP within 15 minutes of receiving the first invoice (402-469-3834).  Some people want to wait and see what they win beofre they decide if they want to pickup or have things shipped.  Declined cards will receive a $15.00 additional charge so make sure your card is good on sign up.  In state bidders needing shipping - (there is no need to call) - if you do not pickup during the pickup time, we will charge your card on file and send a Square Request for the shipping and handling on Thursday or Friday.  ANYONE needing shipping: Please be patient. We sometimes have over 250 invoices needing shipping. ALWAYS CHECK YOUR SPAM FOLDER. IT IS YOUR RESPONSIBILITY TO CHECK YOUR text messages or EMAIL FOR THE SQUARE REQUEST.  There is NO NEED TO CALL (unless you have waited until Friday - March 13th @ 5:30pm and did not receive a Square Request or a message with reason of delay). ALL SQUARE REQUESTS MUST BE PAID BY 5 PM SUNDAY 03/15 @ 5 pm. WE DO NOT HAVE TIME TO CONTACT EACH PERSON, BE RESPONSIBLE AND FOLLOW THE DIRECTIONS HEREIN. Items will ship out in order of payment received on the Square Request. We reserve the right to donate items if the shipping and handling is not paid by 5:00 pm - Sunday the 15th. ***************************************************** 
We DO NOT HAVE OPEN FOR BUSINESS hours We work by appointment only other than the 2 hour pickup time listed above. 
***************************************************** 
Messages or questions?? Call 402-469-3834 to LEAVE A MESSAGE Reply is usually within 24 hours OR Text to the same number for faster reply (usually 2 hours). ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
