title: 2026 Westfalen Verband NA Stallion Service Auction
description:
Welcome to the 2026 Westfalen Verband NA Stallion Service Auction. Bids Open at 1 p.m.. Eastern Friday Feb 27th and begin closing at 7 p.m. Eastern on Sunday March 8th, 2026. Opening bids are set at $500 for Warmblood stallions, $300 for Stallions available with 1 dose of Frozen Semen, and $250 for pony stallions, and the bidding will go up in $25 increments.  Each Stallion's listing has important information regarding the terms of the stallion service donation - whether Fresh Cooled Shipped semen or Frozen, Collection and Shipping fees, and which Stallion Book the stallion is entered in, etc. Please make sure to read carefully through all of the Stallion's information before bidding and visit the stallion owner's website/contact the stallion owner with any questions before you bid.  

Register to bid by clicking on Login/New Bidder at the top right of the page, or you can also click on the green Bidding button, and that will bring you to the sign-up screen. You will then select "Click Here if You are a New Bidder" and follow the instructions on the screen to set up your username and password. If you've already registered to bid for one of our auctions just enter your username and password. If you need help signing up or have any questions please contact the Stallion Service Auction Manager Ann Daum Kustar at 1-605-441-6521 or email office@westfalenverband.com  Bids can NOT be retracted, so it is imperative that you are sure of your bids before they are placed. 

Note - The auction lots begin closing at 7 p.m. Eastern Sunday March 8th. Each lot closes 1 and 1/2  minutes after the lot before. Each lot is set to finish with a Soft Close - which means that if a bid is placed on a stallion in the last 2 minutes of the auction, the time on that lot will extend by another minute. This is to discourage automated sniper programs, which swoop in with a bid in the last seconds of an auction, faster than human reaction time. 

Remember you can place a Max Bid by highlighting over the next bid amount and entering the amount you are willing to pay for the stallion service. The program will bid only up as far as it has to to keep you the high bidder - just like in Ebay. Other bidders won't see your maximum bid.  

Winning bidders will be notified by e-mail at the close of the auction. Please check your e-mail for notification of a win or loss. You must then make payment via paypal, credit card, check or money order to Westfalen NA within seven days from the end of the auction.   

The Westfalen NA is auctioning a donated stallion service contract provided by the Owner shown. Bidders are hereby notified to direct inquiries about this stallion service and any contract issues to the Owner listed.  The winning bidder agrees to abide by terms of the stallion owner's Breeding Contract. Make sure that you familiarize yourself with the specific details for the contract of the stallion you are interested in. If you have any questions about the stallion himself, the additional fees, the duration of the contract, etc., contact the stallion owner directly. Be sure that you know exactly what you are bidding on.  

Stallions are all activated for the 2026 season with Westfalen NA unless otherwise noted. Stallions which are not activated require an additional fee for foal registration at time of inspection. All stallions Stud Book status are listed as either Stud Book I, II or  IIA (eligible for upgrade into Stud Book I after performance results).  Stallions listed as Ib  needs to complete perforamnce results - but are currently still in status in Book I, due to their age and/or completing their 1st performance test, but will need to complete a final test./performance before earning lifetime approval. Foals born to Book I or Ib stallions out of eligible mares will receive full pink papers.  Ib stallions may drop to a lower book in the future so be aware, if not using your breeding in the year you purchased it.  

The Westfalen Verband NA registry represents the Westfälisches Pferdestammbuch e.V. in North America. The breed-name for foals registered with us in Germany as well as in North America is Westfalen or Deutsches Reitpony. 

Foals registered with Westfalen Verband NA get German passports.  For more Information on Westfalen NA's registration papers and brands visit:   http://www.westfalenverband.com

title: Firearms, Silver Coins, Collectables, Lewis Auction Center
description:
Firearms, Silver Coins, Collectables, and more Lewis Auction Center Hodgenville, KY  

ONLINE AUCTION ONLY:  February 23rd, 2026, Thru March 8, 2026, with a soft close at 7:00 P.M.
Go to: Lewisauction.hibid.com to view, register and bid. This is an Online Only Auction

Lewis Auction Company has been commissioned to sell, Firearms, Silver Coins, and Collectables  and more. 

Highlights include: 

"	Firearms in various styles and calibers  
"	Silver Coins
"	NASCAR collectables and vintage toys

Bidding is INTERNET ONLY. Lots will begin to close in order and will close EVERY  20 SECONDS until the auction is completed. This auction will feature extended bidding, meaning that if a bid is placed in the final 2 minutes, the bidding will be extended an additional 2 minutes to ensure that all bidders have time to place their bids. This will continue until there is no bid for a 2-minute period. 
Do not wait till the last minute, your internet speed may determine if the bid is accepted.

No Preview please see photos

If you are sales tax exempt, sales tax will be removed from TERMS: 

Local Checkout/payment pick up: ON March 10th, 2026, from 2:00 P.M. to 6:00 P.M. All Items will be checked out/paid for at Lewis Auction Center 4691 Old Elizabethtown Road Hodgenville Kentucky 42748. Local Firearm purchases will be picked up Sportsman Rod and Gun, 3175 S. Dixie Highway at Sportsman Rod and Gun Elizabethtown KY, with your paid receipt and a $25.00 transfer fee (paid to the gun shop directly) will be charged per firearm (serial number) purchased. Keep this in mind when bidding. All FFL rules will be followed NO REFUNDS do not bid if you are not eligible to buy a firearm. For Ammunition, and other non-FFL items.  You will receive your item when paying (Must be 21 years old to pick up ammunition).

Shipments:

All Firearm, including black powder shipments, will be handled by Sportsman Rod and Gun 270-737-4867 at the expense of the buyer to an FFL dealer in your area a Transfer fee $25.00/packing fee $25.00 for a total of $50.00 (paid to the gun shop directly by the buyer) will be charged plus the actual postal/freight charges this is per serial number shipped. Have your FFL to email a copy of their license to onlinesales@lewisauction.com and FFl@sportsmans-ky.com   we will retain a copy for our records as well as Sportsman's Rod and Gun.

Other shipping: All Non-FFL shipping will be facilitated by Lewis Auction Company. Ammunition shipments will be facilitated by Lewis Auction Company through FedEX with an ORM-D special handling label attached. There will be a minimum charge of $15 per shipment to Lewis Auction company for packing and delivery to the FedEX store in addition to the actual shipping/freight charge billed to your HIBid Credit card on file. Other items will be shipped via USPS or FedEX, and a $10.00 packing fee will apply per invoice, plus actual postage charge. Credit cards will be charged prior to packing and shipping will show as an additional charge. Items we cannot ship may be delivered to UPS Store for pack and ship a $10.00 fee will apply for delivery to the UPS Store you will pay them direct for pack and ship.

Keep in mind per Federal Law FFL firearms must ship alone and cannot be shipped with ammo, and other accessories that did not come in the Firearm box at time of original purchase other than optics thar are mounted.

Additional Terms:

Credit Cards will be charged for any items not picked up and paid for by 6 P.M. March 8th, 2026. 
NOTE: Gold shipments will only be shipped after the certified payment is received. Example: Wire Transfer (immediate after the wire is confirmed), Cashier's Check sent by mail (immediate after receipt), Personal check will be accepted by mail (with a 10 day hold on shipments after bank deposit.)  Credit cards will be charged. A 10% deposit balance will be due by one of the means listed above. Thanks for your understanding but this is for the protection of our consignors and to deter scammers. 

15% buyer's Premium will be added to all bids for Cash or good Check and local pickup. For Credit card transactions a checkout will be provided through Hibid or square plus a 3% surcharge will apply to all credit card/Debit Card transactions. For more information, please contact Auctioneer Barry Brown 270-268-8134, or Broker Bobby Dobson 270-737-1839 for more information, or go to Lewisauction.hibid.com to view, register and bid.

description:

Then, a quick dive into pop music culture with some collectible toys and memorabilia items, sealed card sets, sealed action figures and toys, some vinyl records and 45s and huge groupings of memorabilia, photographs, magazines and more! FOLLOWING THE AUCTION:

Winning bidders will receive both an email and text message with the option to select pickup or shipping.
o If pickup is selected, you'll receive full pickup instructions, including date, time, and location based on the information you provided during registration.
o If shipping is selected, your items will be securely packed by our in-house shipping department. Once packaging is complete, you will receive another email and text message with the total shipping cost and handling fee.
________________________________________
SHIPPING:

Shipping is available on most items and provided in-house by Ben Ladage Auctions using Shipping Saint. After the auction:
"	You will receive an email and text to choose either shipping or pick-up.
"	If shipping is selected, items will be packaged, and you will receive a second notification with shipping and handling costs.
"	We use USPS, UPS, and FedEx and always select the most cost-effective method.
"	Please allow up to 15 business days for items to ship after the pickup date, although most packages go out within 5 business days.
We do not ship large or heavy items unless pre-approved prior to auction closing. We do not accept outside shipping labels or provide shipping quotes. Shipping costs are the responsibility of the buyer and are non-refundable.
________________________________________
ITEM DESCRIPTIONS, INSPECTIONS, & DISCLAIMERS:

All items are sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" with no warranties, guarantees, refunds, or returns of any kind. Descriptions including condition, age, origin, or rarity are offered as opinion only. The bidder assumes all responsibility for determining value and authenticity.
Preview and inspection are strongly encouraged. The auctioneer is not responsible for errors in descriptions or missed bids (online, absentee, phone, or otherwise). All announcements on auction day take precedence over any prior marketing.
________________________________________
PICKUP POLICY - READ CAREFULLY:

All items must be picked up on the scheduled date and time. If you cannot make the scheduled pickup, please do not bid.
Items not picked up are forfeited and become property of Ben Ladage Auctions unless other arrangements have been made in advance.
We understand life happens and will try to work with you-but communication is key. If we have to retrieve your items from the auction site and transport them back to Auburn, a minimum fee of $25 per item will be charged. This fee helps cover time, labor, and tracking logistics.
________________________________________
ABSENTEE BIDDING & TECHNICAL INFORMATION:

Don't want to watch the auction live? The best strategy is to leave your maximum bid ahead of time. Our system will bid incrementally on your behalf.
This is a soft close auction. If a bid is placed in the last 3 minutes of a lot's closing, that lot will automatically extend by 3 minutes. All lots close in staggered intervals.
If you're unsure how to bid online, contact our office before the final day-we're happy to help! Absentee bids are accepted and will include the 15% buyer's premium.
________________________________________

Sellers: Rodenberg Family , John Amberg - POA

We appreciate your bids and support-thank you for participating in this online auction!

title: Vintage Pin-Up Art, Playboy  & Mid Century Americana
description:
Vintage Pin-Up Art, Playboy Collectibles & Mid-Century Americana

A rare and extensive single-collection offering spanning decades of American illustration art and men's magazine history — a must-see event for serious collectors.

Varga & Petty Originals: Dozens of framed works by Alberto Vargas and George Petty, the undisputed masters of mid-century pin-up illustration. Esquire calendar girls, double-sided Varga Varieties of 1946, and large-format framed prints up to 47" — museum-quality pieces at auction prices

Golden Age Playboy Magazines: Rare early issues including October 1955, May 1957, August 1957, and multiple copies from 1958–1962 — the true golden era of Hugh Hefner's vision. Also featured: the legendary August 1965 issue signed by cover model Jo Collins, 1960s runs of 39+ issues, and sought-after celebrity editions featuring Madonna, Marilyn Monroe (7 January 1997 editions), Anna Nicole Smith (9 copies), Bo Derek, and a Playboy: The Parody issue.

Ephemera & Advertising Art: Zippo and novelty lighters (14+), vintage Mennen advertising framed prints, metal advertising signs, pin-up ashtrays, matchbooks, coasters, and steering wheel spinners — perfect for man-cave or mid-century modern décor.Marilyn Monroe Collection: Knives, porcelain dolls, ornaments, playing cards, photographs, and calendars dedicated to the ultimate American icon.

Sealed & Graded-Condition Items: Sealed binder sets of Playboy Centerfold Collector Cards, magazines still in original shipping plastic, and pristine 1970s–1990s runs totaling 500+ issues.

Whether you're a Vargas illustration collector, vintage men's magazine archivist, mid-century Americana enthusiast, or advertising art investor, this auction delivers extraordinary depth .Preview available by appointment only on Friday 3-6-26 call 317-468-3705

*** All items sold as-is, all sales are final.

*** Shipping on ITEMS ONLY****NO FRAMES, CASES OR DISPLAYS WILL BE SHIPPED all items will be removed from frames, cases or displays and they will be disposed of.

title: Chaos Arcade of Spokane Washington LIQUIDATION Online Auctio
description:
ONLINE AUCTION – DON’T MISS OUT! 
'Over the years, Chaos Arcade received significant community support. It was consistently voted as the "Inlander's Best Arcade" and even won "Best Place for a Kid's Birthday Party" one year. The arcade also held successful community events, including blood drives and unique gatherings like the Chaos and Dragons interactive show.' (KHQ News - Daniel Fortin)
 
Chaos Arcade has officially closed its doors as of February 2026, and now ALL inventory is being sold to the highest bidders online!

This is your chance to secure some of the BEST arcade and entertainment inventory of the year — available exclusively through BidderBros.

*Premium arcade equipment and inventory *Rare and high-demand items *Nationwide shipping available via Beltmann Logistics

Whether you're expanding your arcade, upgrading your entertainment venue, or hunting for incredible deals, this auction is packed with opportunity.

All items are located in Spokane, WA — but open to bidders nationwide!

Place your bids now at BidderBros.com

Shipping inquiries: Auction@BidderBros.com

Inventory like this doesn’t come around often — once it’s gone, it’s gone.Start bidding today!

AND join us on Youtube.com/BidderBros the final day of the sale, as items are ending.

title: March Live
description:
March 9th Premier Antique & Decorative Arts Sale Brokenarch Gallery is pleased to present our March 9th auction featuring an impressive assortment of American and Continental antiques, fine decorative arts, and estate treasures. The offering includes Renaissance Revival cabinetry, French gilt metal clocks, Chinese yellow-ground porcelains, American stoneware, early photographic equipment, cast metal weathervanes, folk art sculpture, garden ornaments, architectural salvage, and vintage advertising. Carefully assembled to appeal to collectors, decorators, and design enthusiasts alike, this sale balances strong period examples with distinctive conversation pieces. Each lot reflects craftsmanship, artistry, and the enduring beauty of historic objects ready for the next chapter in their story.

title: The Betty Jane (BJ) Reaves Estate Auction - Part 1
description: Weiss Lake Auction is honored to present the Estate of BJ Reaves at Auction. Mrs. Reaves was originally from Clay County. She graduated college from Jacksonville State University in Education and began teaching in Tucker, GA., later in Montgomery, AL, and finally in Gasden, AL. BJ was passionate about her hobbies. She loved antiques. She had boothes in antique malls for years. Mrs. Reaves also loved lamps and learned to build a new lamp from scratch. Her home has always been filled with lamps and lamp parts. BJ also had a love for gardening and flowers. This is Part 1 of two auctions from her Estate.

title: Vintage Sports Card Spectacular Auction-Latrobe
description:
A Vintage Sports Card Spectacular
This first of two spectacular sports card auctions will present 332 lots of vintage
sports cards from 1964-1974. This auction will have cards of all four major sports
in absolutely amazing condition by the thousands! You will never see cards over
50 years old look like these maybe ever! This first auction is led by the GOAT of
hockey, a stunning Wayne Gretzky Rookie, and many other rookies in excellent to
mint condition such as O.J. Simpson, Terry Bradshaw, Joe Greene, Pete
Maravich, Gayle Sayers, Mike Schmidt, and many more. You will also be able to
bid on thousands of cards of high value such as Wilt Chamberlain, Lew Alcindor,
Pete Rose, Nolan Ryan, Bobby Hull, Gordie Howe, Bart Starr, Joe Namath and
hundreds more! You will also view many lots of commons that will impress you
with their amazing condition from EXCELLENT to MINT!

title: 03-10-2026 Designer Jewelry for Everyday Style
description:
Designer Jewelry for Everyday Style!

Online Auction Tuesday March 10, 2026 
Bid now! www.tomhallauctions.com 

This online auction presents the final offering from a truly superb and expansive jewelry
collection, bringing together an exceptional array of gold, sterling silver, and gemstone collector
pieces. Featured throughout are 10K, 12K, 14K, and 18K gold jewelry alongside beautifully
crafted sterling silver designs. The collection shines with a rich variety of gemstones including
opal, turquoise, diamond, garnet, ruby, blue topaz, aquamarine, citrine, amethyst, and many
more, offering something for every style and taste. Includes necklaces, pendants, earrings, bracelets, brooches, and even some gold watches! 

A standout highlight includes an impressive assortment of Honora genuine cultured pearl sets in
an incredible range of colors, styles, and coordinated pieces. Many items appear never worn,
with original tags still intact, making this a rare opportunity for collectors and gift buyers alike.
The auction features an abundance of rings, necklaces, and bracelets from highly sought-after
designers such as Carolyn Pollack, Barbara Bixby, Kirks Folly, Jay King, Nikki Butler, and Black
Hills Gold.

Noteworthy treasures include a striking 14K yellow gold necklace showcasing a mosaic opal
inlay pendant with polished and twisted detailing, measuring approximately 26mm by 16mm and
suspended from an elegant fancy chain. An exquisite 14K gold bracelet with an intricate wheat
design measures 8 inches in length and offers timeless sophistication. A remarkable Carolyn
Pollack Relios collection necklace crafted in sterling silver spans 36 inches and features lapis,
amethyst, and dark pearl beads, centered by a dramatic two-inch pendant with a secure
clasping bail, highlighted by lapis with an inset amethyst stone framed in ornate silverwork.
Additional highlights include a 14K gold floral ring adorned with opal and amethyst gemstones,
14K gold and lavender jade hoop earrings, a Carolyn Pollack Rodeo sterling silver necklace
from the Relios line, and so much more.

This is the final chance to acquire pieces from this extraordinary jewelry collection, blending fine
materials, respected designers, and exceptional craftsmanship into one unforgettable auction.

Preview the auction by appointment, call 610-799-0808
Items are located at: Tom Hall Auction Gallery, 4644 Main St (Rte 309),Schnecksville, Pa. 18078

Pick up times after the auction: 12PM-3:30PM the day after the auction, 
9:30AM-3:30PM Monday – Friday  within 7 days of the auction.

In-house shipping!  
Bid confidently as we handle the packing and shipping straight from our gallery to you. 

Consider using our professional in-house shipping department for your auction treasures.

With over 30 years of packaging and shipping experience our in-house shipping department has you covered.

For shipping inquiries, contact us at: shipping@tomhallauctions.com.

Online bidding Terms: Major Credit Cards, 20% Buyer’s  for this auction, 6% Sales Tax collected if you have not provided a valid Sales Tax Exemption Certificate.

Payment is by major credit card. Checks or pay pal are not accepted.

(IMPORTANT NOTE: At auctioneers discretion, payment may be by wire transfer or cash for all purchases with an invoice exceeding $1200.00.)  Auctioneer may require a copy of valid ID for any transactions.

title: 76.60 +/- Acres - Town of Lemonweir & Marion Juneau Co.
description:
A blend of nature and opportunity. This 76.60 acre parcel features 61.6+/- tillable acres and 15+/- acres of woods/recreation. Whether you're looking for a farmland investment, a private retreat, or a recreational property, this one has it all. Just a short drive to the interstate, Castle Rock Lake, Mauston, and Wisconsin Dells. A peaceful convenient location to meet all your needs. Call today for a showing!

2025 Taxes = $278.63.

TERMS:
1. The successful bidder will sign a standard Offer to Purchase form directly following the auction. The offer will not have any contingencies of any kind.

2. Earnest money shall be $20,000 and will be collected within 24 hours of the auction close. If the high bid is accepted, the earnest money will be applied toward the purchase price. If the bid is not accepted, the earnest money will be returned to the buyer.

3. The offer is subject to the approval of the owners, who will have until March 12, 2026 to accept, reject, or counter today's high bid.

4. This offer may not be rescinded prior to Midnight of March 12, 2026.

5. In the event of a counteroffer, today's highest bidder will be given the first opportunity to accept any counteroffer. 

6. A Real Estate Condition Report has been provided. The buyer is purchasing the property in its AS IS condition. No warranties are made as to any aspects regarding the property or its uses. Buyers shall rely entirely on their own inspections of the property and the records.

7. If the offer is accepted, closing will take place on or before April 24, 2026 at Gowey Title in Mauston. Occupancy will be given at closing. 

8. Real Estate taxes will be prorated at the time of closing based on the 2025 taxes.

9. Tillable acreages were derived from FSA maps but are not guaranteed.

10. A preliminary title policy has been provided by Gowey Title and is available for review prior to bidding. The buyer accepts the policy as it reads.

11. Any personal property left is left solely at the convenience of the seller and is considered to have no monetary value. 

12. The property will be sold by the surveyed acreage of 76.6 Acres.  The property has been surveyed and a copy has been made available to the buyer.

13. Purchase includes part of Parcel #29018116.4 Town of Lemonweir, Parcel #290260525 Town of Marion, Parcel #290180116.3 Town of Lemonweir.

14. Gavin Bros., LLC is the agent of the seller in this transaction.

15. Per item 15 on the attached title policy (doc 163472), the mineral rights are reserved by Agribank FCB (Successor to The Federal Land Bank of Saint Paul).  This interest will run with the land.

title: March Collectibles Countdown Auction
description:
**There is no in-house bidding.** 

Not sure how to bid through HiBid? Sign-up is quick, easy and secure. We can help walk you through the process by phone or in person. 
The gallery will be open for in-person preview on Tuesday, 3/10 & Wednesday, 3/11, from 10 to 4 PM, and by appointment.

This auction includes a selection of vintage Gen-X toys, including dozens of action figures and play sets – STAR WARS (original trilogy), TMNT (Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles) and dozens of other 1970s-80s treasures.
There are also 200 lots of vintage SALT & PEPPER shakers – thousands of pieces altogether! – including many scarce and highly collectible examples.
This sale also includes many lots of jewelry, coins, sterling silver items and many more exciting collectibles.
 You won’t want to miss browsing through the catalog!
Everything is being sold with no reserve … and a $20 opening bid. Place your bids before the clock runs out!
PLEASE NOTE OUR TERMS REGARDING INVOICES, PAYMENTS AND SHIPPING. When you register to bid, you agree to our terms. Online bidding available exclusively through HiBid. No in-house bidding, phone bids or absentee bids. Winning bidders will be invoiced the week after the auction.  If you are local, please wait to come in until you receive your invoice. Not local? No problem – we can ship some items worldwide and provide you with a list of shippers for the other items. Items we can ship are clearly marked as such in the catalog.
Winners will be invoiced the Thursday after the auction.


If you are local please wait until you receive an invoice before you come to the gallery. Invoices will come from info@blackwellauctions.com

title: VINTAGE & COLLECTABLE DOLLS

This auction features an extensive and diverse collection of vintage and collectible dolls, including:
* Madame Alexander dolls – sought-after classics in various eras and styles
* Girhams dolls – cherished for their whimsical designs

title: 900 Cardinal Rd. Lancaster, PA 17601
description: This three-bedroom brick ranch home is nestled in a quiet residential neighborhood and offers convenient access to Routes 23 and 30. The main level features a spacious family room with an electric brick fireplace, a custom cherry eat-in kitchen, a formal dining area, three bedrooms, a full bathroom, and a linen closet. The partially finished basement provides additional living space with a second family room featuring a bar, a full bathroom, and a large versatile room ideal for storage or additional recreation space. Additional amenities include one-car attached garage, wooden patio, replacement windows, storage shed, full attic, and numerous mechanical upgrades, including a newer oil burner. Don’t miss this great family home located in East Hempfield Township!

title: 3/11/2026 - KISS Legends & Premier Sports Memorabilia
description:
Online-Only Consignment Auction
John T. Henry Auction Co. LLC
3081 Waccamaw Blvd., Myrtle Beach, SC 29579
Wednesday, March 11th at 6PM
________________________________________
Important Shipping Update!!!

All shipping arrangements must now be made through Shipping Saint. After the auction closes, all customers will receive a text message to confirm either their pickup time or their shipping details.

Shipping Saint is now fully integrated with our trusted shipping provider, Carolina Services & Crating. This integration allows the shipping company to receive detailed order information for each customer. They will correspond with you directly via text regarding shipping bill payment, provide tracking information, and keep you updated on the shipping process from start to finish.
________________________________________
Auction Overview

John T. Henry Auction Co., LLC is proud to present The Rock Burch Collection: KISS Legends & Premier Sports Memorabilia Auction — an extraordinary offering from the late William “Rock” Burch.

Mr. Burch was a passionate and lifelong fan of the legendary rock band KISS. Over decades, he traveled extensively across the United States and internationally to attend concerts, exclusive meet-and-greets, and multiple KISS Kruises. His dedication and enthusiasm allowed him to assemble an impressive, highly personal collection of authentic memorabilia.

This auction features:

- Personally signed items by band members past and present
- Rare and highly sought-after stage-played Gene Simmons Axe guitars
- Memorabilia associated with Paul Stanley’s stage microphone
- Autographed collectibles obtained directly through in-person events
- Additional authenticated items acquired from reputable auction houses

Mr. Burch was meticulous about authenticity, ensuring that the signed items within his collection were properly obtained and verified.
________________________________________
Auction Location & Schedule:

3081 Waccamaw Blvd., Myrtle Beach, SC 29579
Preview is available on Sunday, March 8th from 12-3PM or private showings available for out-of-town buyers by contacting 843-698-2776 for show times.
The auction will begin closing on Wednesday, March 11th at 6PM
Item pickup will take place Thursday, March 12th from 10AM-2PM

Buyers will receive a text after the auction with a link to confirm pickup or request shipping. If picking up in person, use the scheduler link provided in the text to reserve a pickup time.
Payment Information:
Credit cards will be charged immediately following the auction for a nonrefundable deposit of up to $2000. The remaining balance must be paid in full on pickup day. Payment is required before any items can be removed.
__________________________________________________
Shipping:
Shipping is available for all items and is handled through Carolina Relocation Services (CRS). If you need shipping, reply to the post-auction text or to your paid-in-full invoice. Items for shipping will be picked up by CRS at our facility and transported to their warehouse at 6720 Elbow Road, Conway, SC 29527. CRS will contact you directly to arrange packing, quotes, and delivery.
jthpackers@oulook.com
843-349-1070
_________________________________________________
Important Terms and Conditions
All items are sold "AS IS, WHERE IS" with no guarantees, refunds, or returns.
A 13% buyer's premium applies to all purchases.
An 8% sales tax will be collected unless a valid retail sales license is on file.
Descriptions are believed to be accurate but are not guaranteed. We make every effort not to mislead any buyer, and we strive to identify any imperfections or counterfeit items. However, we do not catch everything, and any oversight is never intentional. We advise consignors to perform due diligence before submitting items for sale.
Bidders are strongly encouraged to attend preview days for inspection and to call or email with any questions or concerns prior to bidding. By placing a bid, bidders accept the item's condition and agree to all terms. Please bid accordingly.
Item Removal
Buyers are responsible for removing their purchases during the scheduled pickup window.
Items not picked up by the deadline will be considered abandoned. Abandoned items may be resold, and no refunds will be issued. The auction company is not responsible for damage or theft after the auction ends.
Transportation
Buyers must make their own arrangements for transporting items. We do not provide moving services.
Shipping is available through CRS only.
Registration
By registering, bidders agree to all terms and conditions.
Failure to comply may result in the loss of bidding privileges.
__________________________________________________
Final Notes:
New items are accepted for future auctions immediately after this one closes. Space is limited, so all purchased items must be removed on time. We appreciate your cooperation in helping us keep the auction process smooth and organized.

title: International Coins #21
description: Next in the coins series auctions from a local collector who specialized in foreign coins for nearly 50 years.  We tested silver coin using our in-house tools to verify silver content mostly using our XRF tester.  We want to offer as much assurance to bidders as possible that all of these coins are accurate and our seller agreed with this approach to the process.  It has taken some time but we think worth it.  We tried to include Krause or Yeoman numbers for the foreign coins as well as silver content percentage by book.  We think we identified everything correctly but forgive us if we have a typo somewhere in the ID numbers.  Enjoy!

Buyers are solely responsible for removing all items they have won, including items located in basements, garages, outbuildings, or exterior areas.Any items not picked up in full by the end of the final scheduled pickup period will be considered abandoned property. Buyers who fail to remove all of their purchased items will be assessed a Dumping & Disposal Fee to cover labor, handling, disposal, and/or donation costs.	•	Minimum dumping fee: $50.00	•	Additional charges may apply depending on volume, weight, and disposal requirements.The dumping fee will be charged to the buyer’s card on file without further notice. title: Vintage Toys, Cast Iron Banks & Classic Collectibles
description:
This online auction features 376 cataloged lots of cast iron banks, vintage toys, dolls, plush collectibles, games, and children’s books.

Highlights include cast iron mechanical and still banks such as Jonah and the Whale, bull dog and cat banks, along with additional coin banks. Toy offerings include Hubley cast iron toys, Nylint pressed-steel trucks, wind-up toys from Japan and Marx, pull toys, tin toys, cap guns, diecast vehicles, and character-themed toys including Popeye and Snoopy examples.

The auction includes multiple Cabbage Patch Kids dolls (Coleco, 1984), large Raggedy Ann and Andy dolls and related items, Raikes Bears and Steiff plush animals, as well as additional plush and character toys.

Also included are board games and tabletop games such as Monopoly editions, solitaire games, Spear’s Comical Tivoli, and other marble games.

Children’s books are well represented, including vintage and early editions such as Winnie-the-Pooh (1926), Whitman titles, Platt & Munk editions, and grouped book sets.

This is a diverse multi-category collection featuring cast iron banks, pressed steel and tin toys, dolls, plush, character toys, games, and vintage children’s literature.

title: Ousley Farms Retirement - Equipment Auction
description:
Welcome to the Ousley Farms Multi Auction Retirement! A total of 3 auctions to complete this entire farm retirement. Here you are at Auction # 2. Details below. 

Auction # 2 is LIVE In-Person and Online on Thursday, March 12th @ 10 AM EST

Location: 5705 W 1300 N Elwood, IN 46036

Open House for Real Estate, Farm Equipment, & Personal Property: Sunday, March 1st from 1 PM to 5 PM

This auction will include all of the Farm Equipment. Don't miss out on this full lineup of an entire farming operation! 
John Deere Tractors inlcuding a 2014 JD 5065E, 2016 JD 6120R, 2007 JD 7420, 1989 JD 4755, and a 2004 JD 9220. A 2016 John Deere 324K Loader. Excellent lineup of Hay Equipment including a 2022 New Holland BC5070 Square Baler, 2020 JD 450M Round Baler, 2020 JD 630 MoCo, Kuhn Speedrake SR 110 GII, Kuhn GF 5202 Hay Tedder, Out Front Accumulator, and grapple, JD 265 Disc Mower, 2020 JD R280 Disc Mower, and 6 hay wagons! Planting Equipment from a 2003 JD 1690 Soybean Special Drill, Sunflower 9421-30 Grain Drill, JD 1760 12 Row Conservation Planter, and a JD 7000 6 Row Planter. Harvest Equipment including a 2006 JD 9760 STS Combine, 2000 JD 9650 STS Combine, JD 635F Hydraflex Grain Platform, JD 930F Grain Platform, 2008 JD 612C Corn Head, 3 Head Carts, a Demco 850 Grain cart, 4 Gravity Wagons, 2 grain trucks, a semi and a grain trailer. A 2011 Walinga Agri-Vac 6614. A 2022 Chevrolet Silverado 1500, 2007 Regal Fleetwood Exreme Edition Camper, 2022 Forest River Salem Camper, 2016 John Deere 825i Gator, 2008 Trailer Man Gooseneck Trailer, 2018 Load Trail 14ft Dump Trailer, 2011 Trailerman 20ft Tilt Trailer, 2 John Deere 980 field cultivators, Unverferth 1225 28ft Double Basket Rolling Harrow, DMI 26ft Crumbler, 2 DMI 13 Knife Anhydrous Applicators, 2 Patriot 100 Seed Tenders, a 3 box seed tender, 1977 Ford F350 Flatbed Truck, Krause 1900 25ft Disc, John Deere Z960R 60in Mower, John Deere X390 54in Riding Mower, and more!

For Auction 2 - NO Buyers Premium for In-Person bidders! Save money by attending this auction on-site!
Buyers premium varies for online platforms. Use BidBoone.com for 5% BP with $2,500 cap. Use Equipment Facts or HiBid platforms for 7% BP with $7,500 cap.There is no sales tax on this portion of the retirement auction!


Auction # 1 will include two Online Only Auctions for the personal property of this retirement.

Online Only Day 1 is on Wednesday, March 4th @ 5 PM. This auction will include tools, shop items, farm supplies, Matco, Craftsman, John Deere, Dewalt, Ryobi, Pittsburg tools, and more! Wrenches, Sockets, Plyers, Screwdrivers, Tool Boxes, Chainsaws, Bolt Bins, and much more! 

Online Only Day 2 is on Thursday, March 5th @ 5 PM. This auction will include Collectible Coins, Silver Coins, Jewelry, McCoy Pottery, Roseville Pottery, Furniture, Antiques, Precious Moments, Loads of Decorations, Milk Bottles, Huge Beer Can Collection, Vera Bradley, and more!

Auction # 3 - LIVE In-Person and Online on Thursday, March 12th @ Approx. 1 PM EST. 
This auction will follow the conclusion of the Farm Equipment. - An exceptional offering of Real Estate. A total of 80 +/- acres being offered in two tracts.

Tract 1 - 10 +/- Acres with a full list of great features including the 3 Bedroom / 2 Bathroom 2,092 sqft home! Downstairs is over a 1,000 sqft basement that was used for storage, utilities, and extra space! Large primary bedroom with walk in closet and bathroom. Spacious family room just off the kitchen. Stainless appliances with a newer LP Gas Stove and a newer microwave. Spare dining room and an office space just outside 2 other bedrooms. Multiple new upgrades! Within last two years this home has had all new windows and a new roof! The water softener is owned and was upgraded to a quality system in last few years. New vinyl plank flooring throughout part of the house. New carpet in the primary bedroom. The list goes on! The home has a detached 2+ car garage with a large open porch that was used for relaxing and seeing over the large yard and firepit area.

Tract 1 comes with over 80,000 bushels of grain storage! 4 Brock Grain Bins and an additional 2,000 bushel holding bin. The bins are 40,000 Bushels, 20,000 Bushels,12,000 Bushels, and 12,000 Bushels. All 4 bins have air tubes on them currently. There is no shortage of farm storage on this property as well! 2 Morton Barns - 1 has a 36x54 heated shop and 54x63 storage area. The second Morton Barn measures 69x71 - Featuring a loading dock setup and a 17x26 insulated vegetable cooler! There is also a 29x45 Pole Barn and a 24x29 block building.

Tract 2 - 70 +/- Acres of mostly tillable farmland! Approximately 62 acres of tillable ground. The other 8+/- acres is made up of a sparsely wooded pasture space that has been used for cattle and hay over the years. As well as an approximately 2 acre pond. The Ousley's have farmed this ground for the last 50+ years that they have owned it. Wayne said he has never lost a crop on this ground due to standing water. It drains extremely well with the Chalfant Ditch that runs through the entire property. The county just put in an all new outlet on the south end of the farm.

There is 46 acres of wheat planted on Tract 2. The buyer of Tract 2 will have the right to harvest the wheat crop and split the gross vlaue of the wheat sold 50/50 with the seller. The right to bale the leftover straw has been assigned. The buyer of Tract 2 will receive $2,000 after the straw has been baled from the seller. Once the wheat and straw have been removed the buyer will have full access to that part of the farmground to do as they want. The buyer will have possession of the other tillable ground that has not been planted at closing. 

Reach out with any questions or to schedule a time to view the real estate!Caleb Cleek (765)635-9861

1131 Cherry Springs Ln., Trade TN 37691
This home is a 3-bedroom, 2-bathroom new construction. Spanning 1,388 sq ft, this thoughtfully designed home has high-end custom details, including granite countertops and cherry wood kitchen cabinets. Perfectly situated for outdoor enthusiasts, located just 20 minutes from Boone and 1 hour from the Appalachian Trail.
Top Features:
·        1,388 sq ft | 3 Beds | 2 Baths | 2 Stories
·        +/- 1.09 acres
·        High-End Finishes: Granite countertops, custom chocolate cherry cabinets, and Smartcore Blue Ridge Pine flooring.
·        Luxury Baths: Primary suite includes a custom rain shower with body jets and porcelain trim.
·        Layout: Versatile floor plan with dual primary suites (main & upper), a loft nook, and cathedral ceilings.
·        Infrastructure: HVAC heat pump, 200 Amp panel, Spectrum internet, and a fully encapsulated crawlspace with a heavy-duty dehumidifier.
·        Exterior: Large porch with field-wire railing, seasonal mountain views, and wildlife-rich surroundings.
·        Location: 20 mins to Boone; 1 hour to Appalachian Trail.
·        Crawl Space: Fully encapsulated crawlspace
Appliances will convey with the sale of the home. Other furnishing are available for purchase with the sale of the home for an additional $10,000.
Online Auction Ends Thursday, March 12th, 2026 at 3 PM
1131 Cherry Springs Ln., Trade TN 37691
PREVIEW: February 21st at 10 AM
CONTACT: Broker & Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at (540) 239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com. You can also contact Broker Sharon Roseman at (828) 320-4726 or email SharonCRoseman@gmail.com with United Country Big6 Properties.
DIRECTIONS: From Boone - Head west on King Street turning onto 421 North King Street. Go 14 miles on Highway 421 passing the state line. Turn right on Cherry Springs Lane. Take the first right and the home will be on the right.
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 3 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating TN State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property information.

Close on or before April 27th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the Seller up to, but not beyond the Seller’s reserve price. Auction starts ending at 4 PM on March 12th, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. title: +/-0.87 ac & Mountain Retreat – 1135 Cherry Springs Ln.
description:
Are you searching for a new construction home located in the Appalachian Mountains. This newly constructed home features 3 bedrooms, 2 bathrooms and breathtaking seasonal views of the Appalachian Mountains.
1135 Cherry Springs Ln., Trade TN 37691
This home is a 3-bedroom, 2-bathroom new construction. Spanning 1,415 sq ft, this thoughtfully designed home has high-end custom details, including granite countertops and cherry wood kitchen cabinets. Perfectly situated for outdoor enthusiasts, located just 20 minutes from Boone and 1 hour from the Appalachian Trail.
Top Features:
·        1,415 sq ft | 3 Beds | 2 Baths | 2 Stories
·        +/- 0.87 acres
·        High-End Finishes: Granite countertops, custom chocolate cherry cabinets, and Smartcore Blue Ridge Pine flooring.
·        Luxury Baths: Primary suite includes a custom rain shower with body jets and porcelain trim.
·        Layout: Versatile floor plan with dual primary suites (main & upper) and cathedral ceilings.
·        Infrastructure: HVAC heat pump, 200 Amp panel, Spectrum internet, and a fully encapsulated crawlspace with a heavy-duty dehumidifier.
·        Exterior: Large porch with field-wire railing, seasonal mountain views, and wildlife-rich surroundings.
·        Location: 20 mins to Boone; 1 hour to Appalachian Trail.
·        Crawl Space: Fully encapsulated crawlspace
Appliances will convey with the sale of the home including: refrigerator, electric range, dishwasher, microwave, propane gas logs, and washer and dryer.
Online Auction Ends Thursday, March 12th, 2026 at 4 PM
1135 Cherry Springs Ln., Trade TN 37691
PREVIEW: February 21st at 10 AM
CONTACT: Broker & Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at (540) 239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com. You can also contact Broker Sharon Roseman at (828) 320-4726 or email SharonCRoseman@gmail.com with United Country Big6 Properties.
DIRECTIONS: From Boone - Head west on King Street turning onto 421 North King Street. Go 14 miles on Highway 421 passing the state line. Turn right on Cherry Springs Lane. Take the first right and the home will be on the right.
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 4 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
SIMULTANEOUS CLOSE OF LOT BIDDING: Bidders desiring more than one offering will need to be the high bidder on all offerings desired. Each offering will stay open until all bidding is complete, and all offerings will close simultaneously.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating TN State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property information.
TERMS & CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $5,000 is required on the day of sale. Close on or before April 27th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the Seller up to, but not beyond the Seller’s reserve price. Auction starts ending at 4 PM on March 12th, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. Be sure to register to bid early to ensure you receive notifications regarding any changes to the Auction, Bidder Packet and/or Pre-Auction offers received. Download Bidder Information Pack for complete terms and conditions, purchase contract and maps at BidBig6.com

title: Hoover Model Train Collection & Sports Cards
description:
Online Toy Train, Sports Card & Collectibles Auction
Preview: Wednesday, March 11th | 4:00–5:00 PM
Closes: Thursday, March 12th starting at 6:00 PM
Pickup: Friday, March 13th | 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM
Preview Location: Walton Auction Co. Office
227 W. Wyandot Ave., Upper Sandusky, OH

Walton Auction Co. is excited to present an outstanding collection of vintage Lionel, American Flyer, Williams, Hafner and Kusan O-Gauge trains, along with a large assortment of sports card sets, memorabilia, and collectibles.

This sale features a strong lineup of post-war Lionel steam locomotives, pre-war pieces, rare accessories, and hard-to-find engines, plus sealed baseball card sets from the late 1980s and early 1990s.

Featured Train Highlights:
•	Lionel 736 Berkshire Steam Engine with 2466 coal car
•	Lionel 675 & 736 Steam Engines with coal tenders
•	Lionel 238E & 224E Steam Engines with matching coal cars
•	Lionel Pre-War Locomotive Model 253
•	Lionel 225E Engine & Tender
•	Lionel 2020 Engine with 2020W Pullman (B&O 675)
•	Lionel 6460 Bucyrus Erie Crane Cars (multiple)
•	Lionel 6440 Pullman & 6441 Observation Cars
•	Pair of Lionel 226E Engines with 2666W Coal Cars
•	Pair of Lionel 2343 Engines with 2343C Car
•	Lionel 623, 651, 1666, 2055, 2056, 229, 224 & 2025 Engines
•	Hafner Wind-Up Engines & Cars
•	Williams Delaware & Hudson Engines (802 & 804)
•	Large assortment of Lionel cabooses, log carriers, spotlight cars, animal carriers, crane cars, military sets, and accessories
•	Extensive track lots, switches, transformers, and control equipment
Whether you're a serious Lionel collector or looking to build or expand a layout, this auction offers depth and variety across multiple eras.
 
Sports Card & Memorabilia Highlights:
•	Multiple Upper Deck 1989 Complete Sets
•	Upper Deck 1990 & 1991 Factory Sealed Sets
•	Score 1990 Collector Sets (704-card sets) – several unopened
•	Fleer 1987, 1988, 1989, 1990 Sets (sealed and opened)
•	Topps 1987, 1989, 1990 Baseball Sets
•	Donruss 1988–1992 Sets
•	Football & multi-sport sets including Score 1989 (Sanders & Aikman rookies)
•	Sports posters (40” x 26”), including Notre Dame, vintage baseball & college basketball
•	First Day Stamp Sets & collectible stamp lots

This is a well-rounded collector’s sale with strong representation of post-war Lionel steam power, pre-war pieces, crane cars, passenger cars, and factory card sets from the peak collecting era.
Join us for preview on March 11th and get your bids in before bidding closes Thursday night at 6 PM!

Bid online.

Owner: Wayne Hoover
Terms include a 15% buyer’s premium. 
Samuel R. Baer, Auctioneer
Call: (330) 424-8336
Office: (419) 294-0007
Email: sam@samuelrbaer.com

title: 1994 Spartan II Luxury Show Car
description:
Automotive designer Ron Sparks, designed the Spartan II and made 500 prototypes.  Due to the lack of funding, Mr Sparks sold the rights of the car to luxury auto builder Ned Lamouteaux.

THE SPARTAN II is not a kit car
.  The cars were built in a small factory in San Marcos, Ca. Styled from a 1934 Mercedes 340K and Took 5 Weeks to Build. Spartan's are built on a 1987 Nissian 300ZX platform.

THIS IS A CUSTOM MADE CAR
 There are only 15 coast of paint on the car.6 Gold plated hub caps, and leather interior.  This particular vehicle retailed for 90,000.00 ( they are the thrid owners). 
Because of the large expense, Spartan's were discontinued in 1996.  According to Ron Sprak's son Terry there were only 500 Spartan's built with this being 1 of the last 6 built in 1994

A FEW CELCBRITIES WHO OWNsPARTANS INCLUDED
Joan Collins, Eddie Murphy, Larry Holmes and Tony Doresett. It has benn on shows such as Dallas, Flamingo Road, Matt Houston, and 101 Dalmation's look alike.
A Sheik even bought 2 to go on his American Express Card.  
As of Today , only 15 of these cars are know to exist.

This vehicle was featured on WCIA's "Our Town Rossville" onJuly 1st, 2016
The Vehicle was driven frequently.


This car has been to many car shows, featured on the news many times.  the owner even dressed like Corella De Vil.  The owners have had a blast with this car and now it is time for a new owner.

It has been maintained and garage kept.  Clean Title

All information was derived from the current owners, the seller has the right to accept or reject all bids

title: 3/12 John & Anna Sisk Auction Coyle OK
description:
9.04± Acres Property Near Guthrie, OK
John & Anna Sisk Auction
Thursday March 12th 6PM Timed Auction
12775 E College Ave Coyle OK 73027
Directions: From the junction of Highways 33 and 105, located just east of Interstate 35 on the east side of Guthrie, take Highway 105 seven miles east to Choctaw Road. Proceed north on Choctaw Road for one mile, then head east on E0780 Road for one block. Enter the property from the south. Look for LRA signs marking the entrance.
5% Buyers Premium will be added to final bid price.
This property comprises approximately 9.04± acres, located just one mile off Highway 105 and seven miles east of Interstate 35, making it easily accessible. The property sits minutes east of Guthrie and I-35, featuring several buildings, including a partially constructed concrete house. It is ideally suited for various commercial uses. This location could be used residentially as well. A small creek and lots of trees provide a bit of privacy. 
Legal Description: 12-16N-01W NW4 NW4 NW4 LESS 1 ACRE FOR SCHOOL LAND
Annual taxes are $379, with a $1,000 exemption applied.
Detailed Property Description
According to the county assessor, the tract consists of approximately nine acres. The access road from Highway 105 to the property is gravel, and the site includes several structures that make the location suitable for commercial development. The seller previously began construction on a concrete house, with specific details on its structure provided upon request. Quonset Round Top approximately 3000 square feet along with a couple of other shops that have electricity. 

Possession will be granted at closing. This sale includes surface rights only; no mineral rights will be conveyed.

The water well is located near the school property line and was drilled prior to the property's acquisition.
A previous house on the site was destroyed by fire, and its remnants were buried, resulting in elevated ground in certain locations.

title: Lakefront Home for Sale at Auction
description:
This well-maintained two-story home on Claytor Lake features a spacious and functional layout with 4 bedrooms, 2 full baths, 2 half baths, and multiple living and entertaining areas. The main level includes a large open kitchen with extensive cabinetry, oversized center island with seating, stainless steel appliances, breakfast nook, formal dining area, living room, sunroom, and 2 bedrooms (one currently suitable as an office). The layout allows for comfortable main level living if desired. It has +/- 3,795 sq ft of living space, with +/- 2,520 on the main level and +/-1,275 on the upper level. Square footage is derived from physical inspection and scanned 3D tour. Home has been used as an AirBnB in the past, with an average rental rate of $396/night.
The second floor offers a large primary bedroom suite with vaulted ceiling, walk-in closets, private bath, an additional oversized bedroom, a full bath, and open-to-below views overlooking the foyer.
Exterior features include a stone and wood finish, paved driveway, whole house generator, detached 2 car garage with stone accents, rear deck, and large patio area ideal for outdoor entertaining. The property also includes access to Claytor Lake, providing convenient enjoyment of boating, fishing, and waterfront recreation. Home sits on +/- 5.56 acres.
The primary dock is a two-story wooden dock that includes an upper sun deck with lounge seating and a lower level designed for boat and kayak storage. A secondary lower deck provides additional space.
The concrete walkway and a series of wooden stairs, all reinforced by heavy-duty stone retaining walls, go to the lake. The entire path is lined with black metal fencing and decorative stone pillars. This layout provides access between the home and the shoreline.
Property is being sold with a 1-year Home Warranty, Standard Plan, which covers heating & air, plumbing, electrical, appliances, and much more.
A shared driveway and associated right-of-way (ROW) provide access to neighboring properties. A portion of this shared access goes through this property. The shared driveway serves as access for the following properties: 3555 Ferncliff Rd, 3545 Ferncliff Rd, 3537 Ferncliff Rd, 3533 Ferncliff Rd, 3521 Ferncliff Rd, and 3515 Ferncliff Rd. Refer to maps in Bidder Pack and Road Maintenance Agreement for location of shared drive.
A drain field easement serving 3533 Ferncliff Road is located at the northwest corner of this property. See map and VA Health Department docs in Bidder Pack.
This property has a road that goes down to the lake and dock areas. A portion of this road goes through neighbor’s property located at 3533 Ferncliff Road. There is a right of way for that road to access dock and lake. 
Property is subject to road maintenance agreement with an annual fee of $200. There may be an additional fee if emergency repairs are needed. refer to road maintenance agreement in bidder pack. Property is also subject to covenants and restrictions. Refer to Covenants and Restrictions in bidder pack. 
Property Features:
- 4 bedrooms
- 2 full baths
- 2 half baths
- +/- 3,795 Sq Ft
- +/- 5.56 acres
- Waterfront on Claytor Lake
- Primary bedroom suite with walk-in closets
- Open kitchen with large island
- Breakfast nook and formal dining area
- Living room and sunroom
- Bedroom/office on main level
- Open foyer with open-to-below upper level
- Rear deck and large patio
- Detached two-car garage
- 2 story dock
Online Auction Ends Friday, March 13th at 3 PM
3537 Ferncliff Rd., Pulaski, VA 24301
PREVIEW DATE: Thursday, February 19th at 4 PM
CONTACT: Auctioneer Matt Gallimore at 540-239-2585 or email Gallimore.Matt@gmail.com.
DIRECTIONS: From Pulaski, take E Main St towards I-81, turn Left on Old Route 100, take the next Right on to Lowman’s Ferry Rd, turn left onto Ferncliff Rd/Tate Farm Rd just before the bridge, property will be on your left. See signs.
BIDDER PACK: Download Bidder Pack for complete information. Bidder Pack has maps, complete terms and conditions, sample contracts, tax cards, and restrictions. Some of the plats in Bidder Pack may have notations and boundaries created by Auctioneer for illustration purposes, however unchanged surveys are in Bidder Pack as well.
SOFT CLOSE: If a bid is received within the last 2 minutes of the auction or after 3 PM, the auction close time will automatically extend 2 minutes to allow other bidders an opportunity to competitively bid prior to the auction closing. This feature eliminates “snipers” and encourages fair and impartial bidding from all participants.
BUYER’S PREMIUM – TEN PERCENT (10%): Buyer’s Premium shall be added to the final online bid, which will determine the Total Contract Sales Price. Bidders hereby acknowledge and understand that the Buyer’s Premium will be added to the winning bid to create the Total Contract Sales Price for which they are obligated to pay for the property. Example: (winning online bid $100,000 + 10% buyer’s premium = total purchase price of $110,000). Buyer’s Premium is not due until closing.
PRE-AUCTION SALES: As an agent for the Seller, the Auctioneer must present any and all bona fide written offers to the Seller, which may be created outside of the online bidding platform. Therefore, all properties are subject to pre-auction sales. Pre-auction offers must meet all off the auction terms and conditions and must be submitted to the Auctioneer on the Auction Real Estate Sales Contract, along with the required earnest deposit. Properly submitted offers will be presented to the Seller in a timely manner. Seller may accept or reject such offer at their sole and absolute discretion. When a pre-auction offer is submitted, all properly registered online bidders will be notified that “an offer” has been submitted and on which specific property, however the amount of the offer shall remain confidential. All pre-auction offers must allow a minimum of 24 hours for seller’s acceptance. A Referral Fee of 2% of the high bid is offered to a cooperating VA State Licensed Real Estate Salesperson/Broker on any pre-auction offers that are properly completed and submitted on the appropriate forms provided. Download Bidder Pack for Online Bidder’s Agreement, maps, sample contracts, and property information.
TERMS & CONDITIONS: 10% Buyer’s Premium will be added to final high bid to arrive at contract price. Earnest Money Deposit of $10,000 is required on the day of sale. Close on or before April 27th, 2026. Subject to seller confirmation. Property sold as is, where is with any and all faults. Buyer is expected to complete their own due diligence before placing a bid. The Auctioneer is allowed to bid on behalf of the seller up to, but not beyond the Seller's reserve price. Auction starts ending at 3 PM on March 13th, 2026. Please note that during the last two minutes of bidding, if a new bid is placed, the clock is automatically extended an additional two minutes. Be sure to register to bid early to ensure you receive notifications regarding any changes to the Auction, Bidder Packet and/or Pre-Auction offers received. Download Bidder Information Pack for complete terms and conditions, purchase contract and maps at BidBlueRidge.com

title: 3/14/26 March Coin, Jewelry, Stamp & Union Case Auction
description:
March Coin, Jewelry, Stamp & Union Case Auction 

**NOTE: THIS AUCTION WILL CLOSE AT 9AM ***

Auction Highlights Include:
U.S. & Foreign Gold; B.U. Silver Dollars; Type Coins; U.S. & Foreign Currency; Jewelry; Diamonds; Gold; Sterling Silver, Stamps, Sportscards, Important Union Case Collection! 

Auction Ends: Saturday, March 14, 2026 at 9:00AM

Preview In-Person: Friday, March 13, 2026 from 2:00 PM to 5:00 PM at Hessney Auction Center

Staggered Closing: This auction features a staggered closing. The auction will begin to close at 9:00AM, with 20-second intervals between the closing of each lot. The closing time for each lot will be automatically extended by 2 minutes if there is active bidding in the final 2 minutes. 

Payment Terms: After the auction ends on Saturday, Hessney Auction will charge the credit card used to register for the auction.

Important: Bidders with Gold purchases or total purchases exceeding $1,000 for Jewelry, Coins, Sports Cards, and Stamps may be required to provide payment in certified funds unless a previous standing arrangement exists with Hessney Auction Co.

Invoices will be emailed to all winning bidders.
Reminder: Please check your spam folder if you do not see your invoice in your inbox.

Pickup Information:
Pickup Dates & Times: Monday and Tuesday after the auction from 9:30 AM to 5:00 PM

Location: Hessney Auction Center, 2747 Rt 14 N. Geneva, NY 14456

All items must be picked up during these hours. If you are unable to pick up your items during these times, please contact us in advance to make alternative arrangements.

Shipping Terms:
You will receive an email or text from Hessney Auction Co. and Shipping Saint with a link to select "Shipping" or "Pick Up."  If you choose "Shipping," our staff will gather your items. Once your items are ready for shipment, you will receive a notification.When your items are packed, you will receive a final notification with the shipping cost, and you will need to pay for the shipping label. You will also be provided with a tracking number.

Handling Fees: A handling fee of $5 to $10 per package will apply.
USPS Priority boxes: $5 flat fee for handling.
For larger or non-Standard boxes, the handling fee may be up to $10.

Shipping Costs: Shipping costs are based on USPS or UPS rates, plus the handling fee mentioned above.

Please Note: We handle hundreds of packages weekly with a small staff. While we make every effort to ship promptly, please be patient as we work to keep shipping costs low and ensure your items arrive safely. 

Important Notice: You must read and agree to all the terms and conditions before placing any bids. All buyers muct Accept and click "Yes" to confirm your agreement before submitting your bid. Full terms and conditions are available in the Auction Details section under "Terms and Conditions."

THERE IS NO SCHEDULED PREVIEW FOR THIS AUCTION. IF YOU WOULD LIKE TO VIEW ANY OF THE ITEMS AN APPOINTMENT CAN BE MADE BY CALLING 240-347-4908.

Payments accepted will be Master Card, Visa, Discover. WE DO NOT ACCEPT AMERICAN EXPRESS.  There is a Buyer's Premium of 15% and 6% MD sales tax added to all purchases.
Maryland tax will be charged on all purchases unless you have a Valid MARYLAND Tax number provided prior to the close of the auction and spend over $200 before buyer's premium. 
NO OUT OF STATE TAX EXEMPT NUMBERS WILL BE ACCEPTED. IF YOU ARE NOT OK WITH THIS PLEASE DO NOT BID!!

CHECKOUT WILL BE AT THE END OF THE AUCTION. BILLED TO THE CARD ON FILE WHEN YOU REGISTERED WITH HIBID.

WHEN SHIPPING IS OFFERED WE WILL ONLY SHIP TO THE BILLING ADDRESS SHOWN ON THE ACCOUNT. THE REASON FOR THIS IS ALL OF THE FRAUD THAT EXISTS. WE HAVE TO PROTECT OURSELVES FROM RECEIVING CHARGEBACKS ON CREDIT CARDS. THIS HAS BECOME A REAL PROBLEM. IF YOUR ADDRESSES DON'T MATCH PLEASE DO NOT BID. NO EXCEPTIONS!!

NO SHIPPING TO CANADA OR OUTSIDE THE US.

SHIPPING WILL BE AVAILABLE FOR THIS AUCTION. 
THERE WILL BE A CHARGE OF $2.00 PER LOT FOR SHIPPING.
IF YOU ARE NOT OK WITH THIS PLEASE DO NOT BID.

THIS COVERS THE MATERIALS USED - BUBBLE WRAP & BOXES AND THE LABOR TO PACKAGE THE ITEMS FOR SHIPPING. IF YOU DO NOT AGREE TO THIS PLEASE DO NOT BID!!

THIS AUCTION WILL CLOSE BEGINNING SATURDAY, MARCH 14 @ 7:OO PM

THERE WILL BE ONE PICK-UP TIME FOR THESE ITEMS:
SUNDAY, MARCH 15, 2026 FROM 11 AM TILL 2 PM. 
PICK UP LOCATION IS 10228 GOVERNOR LANE BLVD, UNIT 3001 WILLIAMSPORT MD 21795

IF ITEMS ARE NOT PICKED UP BY 2 PM THE DAY OF PICKUP THEY WILL BE CONSIDERED ABANDONED AND BECOME PROPERTY OF THE AUCTION HOUSE. WE DO NOT HAVE REGULAR HOURS IN THE OFFICE & IT IS DIFFICULT TO ARRANGE AN ALTERNATE PICK-UP TIME.
IF YOU CAN NOT PICK UP ON SCHEDULED DATE & TIME PLEASE DO NOT BID.

title: 03-15-2026 Important Fine Art Auction
description:
Important Fine Art Auction!

Online Auction  7PM Sunday, March 15, 2026
Bid Now! www.tomhallauctions.com

This curated online auction presents an outstanding collection of fine art that spans mediums,
movements, and generations of artistic mastery. The offering includes oil, acrylic, and watercolor
paintings alongside sculptures, expressive sketches, pencil and charcoal works, pastels, and
richly layered mixed media pieces. Both framed and unframed works are available, allowing
collectors flexibility in presentation and investment. Styles range from Impressionistic and
abstract compositions to realism, still life, landscape, and beyond, creating a dynamic and
visually compelling catalog.

Highlights include works by William Hugh Ferguson, celebrated for his refined academic
technique and sensitive portraiture, with a special emphasis on his beautifully executed
sketches that reveal his mastery of form, light, and human expression. These works on paper
offer a rare glimpse into Ferguson’s creative process, capturing movement and emotion with
remarkable economy and precision. The auction also features Western watercolor paintings by
Allen M. Dean, known for his atmospheric depictions of frontier life and expansive landscapes
that embody the spirit of the American West.

Sculptural works by internationally recognized artist Steve Tobin bring bold organic forms and
powerful presence, while pieces by Aldro T. Hibbard reflect the rich tradition of American
Impressionism with luminous color and dynamic brushwork. Works by Zvi Mairovich offer
striking contemporary abstraction with energetic texture and movement, complemented by the
refined modernist sensibilities of Klaus Ihlenfeld. The collection further includes compelling
works by Stephen George Maniatty, Hans Moller, and noted American painter William Langson
Lathrop, whose landscapes helped shape early 20th-century Impressionism in the United
States.

Rounding out the auction is a finely detailed KPM hand-painted portrait plaque, representing the
renowned German porcelain tradition prized for its craftsmanship and artistic excellence.
Together, this diverse and thoughtfully assembled collection offers something for every collector,
from museum-quality names to expressive works full of character and history. Whether you are
seeking a timeless classic, a striking contemporary piece, or rare artist studies, this auction
provides an exceptional opportunity to acquire beautiful and meaningful art.

Aldro Thompson Hibbard (American, 1886–1972)
Prominent Rockport School painter celebrated for bold impasto New England winter landscapes and one of the leading Massachusetts Impressionists.

Steve Tobin (American, b. 1957)
Internationally recognized contemporary sculptor known for monumental bronze, glass, and steel works exploring nature and growth forms, including his well-known Root series exhibited in museums and public spaces worldwide.

William Langson Lathrop (American, 1859–1938)
Important founding member of the New Hope Art Colony; American Impressionist known for atmospheric Pennsylvania river landscapes and seasonal rural views.

Hans Moller (American, 1857–1925)
German-born American painter active in Philadelphia and New Hope circles, recognized for Tonalist landscapes and coastal scenes with moody, subdued palettes.

William H. Ferguson (American, 1850–1923) (over 100 pieces from this artist)
Pennsylvania Impressionist-associated landscape painter active in Philadelphia, noted for atmospheric rural and river scenes influenced by late-19th-century American tonalism.

Klaus Ihlenfeld (German-American, 1934–2005)
Sculptor and designer known for cast-bronze furniture and sculptural forms built from dense, organic clusters resembling reeds and natural growth. Active in the United States in the late 20th century, his work blends modernist design with fine art and is collected as both decorative art and sculpture.

Plus many additional listed and unlisted artists – original oils, lithographs, and framed works – offering collecting opportunities in every price range.

Preview by appointment, call 610-799-0808

Items are located at Tom Hall Auction Gallery, 4644 Rt. 309, Schnecksville, Pa. 18078

Pick up hours at the gallery following the auction:  12noon-3:30pm the day after the auction and Monday thru Friday from 9:30AM-3:30PM  daily.

All items MUST be removed within 7 DAYS of the auction.

Local deliver available. Please call us at 610-799-0808 for information.

Online Only Bidding: Auction starts closing with 2 items closing per minute, bidding continues as auction closes.Extended bidding, if a bid is placed within the last 1 minute of a lot’s scheduled close time the bidding will be extended 1 minute from the last bid.

Buying at auction is not like buying new; the items are used, vintage and antique. They are consigned by estates and people downsizing. They have been used in people’s homes for their purposes, so while most items are in good condition, most are not in perfect condition. We provide private previews for the public to come to the gallery and physically inspect items being sold. If you cannot be here for the inspection, please contact us by email for additional photos or more detailed condition reports.

We try to present items to bidders fairly; however, it is the bidder’s duty to make independent investigation of the condition of items before bidding.

Online bidding Terms:  Major Credit Cards, 20% Buyer’s Premium, 6% Sales Tax collected if you have provided a valid Sales Tax Exemption Certificate. 

IMPORTANT PLEASE READ PAYMENT TERMS FOR THIS AUCTION 

All major credit cards are accepted. Your credit  card will immediatley be charged for all purchases. 

However at auctioneers discretion INVOICES EXCEEDING $1200 may required payment by wire transfer or cash.
